steps.n: Changed from basic root to past tense 'he came' by adding fathas and changing ي to ى at the end
Added alif maksura marker (small alif) to show the proper pronunciation of the final ى
Added vertical fatHa mark to indicate a slight lengthening of the final sound when continuing to the next word
Changed from verb root (to command) into noun (a command) by adding vowel marks
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in أَتَىٰٓ (came)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
This special word for Allah comes from adding ال (the) and changing the form slightly. The doubled ل and special spelling show this is a unique name for The One True God, not just any deity.
This is a basic negation particle that means 'not' or 'do not'

steps.m: so not (connected to أَتَىٰٓ أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result of what came before - Allah's command has come, so do not hasten it

steps.m: to rush or do something quickly

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding است at the beginning shows someone is trying to make something happen
Adding ت at the beginning changes who is doing the action from 'he' to 'you'
Adding و at the end makes it plural - now means multiple people rushing

steps.m: you all try to rush it
steps.n: Adding هُ shows what is being rushed, and adding the vowel marks helps with pronunciation

steps.m: to swim, float, or move freely (which led to meaning of being free from imperfection)

steps.n: Root letters connected with vowels to make a noun meaning 'being perfect/flawless'
Adds ان to make it stronger - like saying REALLY perfect/pure
Changed to show we are declaring something's perfection - the stretched alif makes it longer when we say it to emphasize the praise
Added pronoun هُ meaning 'His' referring back to Allah mentioned earlier in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks and the weak letter و changes to ا at the end
Added تَ at the start to show 'he made himself' do the action, and changed ending to ى because it's a special type of verb that ends in ى

steps.m: and He is exalted (above عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ - what they associate with Him)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to mean 'and'. The alif is written as ٰ to show it's elongated. This connects to what follows in the verse about Allah being above what people associate with Him.
This is a basic preposition meaning 'from' or 'away from'
Added the question word ما (what) after عن (from) to ask 'from what?'
The ن from عن combines with the م from ما to make a stronger م sound (shown by the ّ mark). This is like how 'in my' sounds like 'immy' when said quickly in English.

steps.m: to make something a partner or equal with something else

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fatha) between the root letters to make it mean 'he partnered/associated' in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to those who associate partners with Allah in worship.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: to send down repeatedly or gradually
steps.n: Adding shadda to middle root letter shows the action is done repeatedly or with intensity (sending down multiple things or in stages)

steps.m: he sends down repeatedly (angels with revelation)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start. The middle letter now takes kasra. This word shows Allah repeatedly sending down angels with revelation.

steps.m: to send messages or act as a messenger

steps.n: Changed root letters into a noun meaning 'messenger' by adding the vowels a-a
Added ال to make it specific/definite
Added َ to show it's the object of the sentence

steps.m: to flow, move freely (like breath or wind)

steps.n: Forms noun meaning 'spirit/soul' from root concept of flowing/moving freely like breath
Adds 'the' to specify a particular spirit
Shows this word is being acted upon by something else in the sentence
Adds 'with' to show how the angels are being sent down. This word is connected to يُنَزِّلُ (he sends down) in this ayah.

This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'His servants' (عِبَادِهِ) with 'whom He wills' (يَشَآءُ), showing which group is being selected from.
Changed from basic root meaning 'command' into the noun form by adding marks (sukun and fatha)
The word gets a kasra (i) sound at the end because مِنْ owns/possesses it
Added هِ at the end to show it belongs to him
This is the basic form meaning something high or elevated
Adding ى changes this from a concept of height into a preposition showing something is on top of or about something else. Here it connects to 'whom He wills' (مَن يَشَآءُ)
The small alif (ٰ) is added to show this is a special form of ى that stays as 'aa' sound at the end instead of changing to 'ee'
This is a relative pronoun that refers to people. In this ayah, it refers to whichever people Allah chooses from His servants, as it's the subject of يَشَآءُ (He wills/chooses).
Adding the fatha (َ) at the end makes it a complete past tense verb
Adding يَ at the start changes it from past to present tense

This is a basic preposition showing that the command/matter (أَمْرِهِۦ) comes from or originates from Allah. In this ayah, it's describing how the angels are sent down with the spirit from His command.
Added a sukun (ْ) on the ن to show where to stop if you pause on this word when reading. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with basic vowels to form singular noun
Changes singular 'servant' to plural form showing multiple servants
Added kasra to show this word belongs to something (comes after مِنْ meaning 'from/of')
Added هِ (hi) at the end to show these are Allah's servants, like saying 'His servants'
This is a connecting word that joins two ideas together. Here it connects 'He sends down' (يُنَزِّلُ) with what the angels are supposed to say/do 'warn' (أَنذِرُوٓا۟)

steps.m: to warn or alert about danger

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Removed first vowel and changed middle vowel to 'i' to make it a command (like 'you warn!')
Added أَ (a) at the start to make it a command to one person - like saying 'you warn!'
Added وا with damma to show this is a command for multiple people. This command relates to those mentioned in يَشَآءُ (whom He wills) in the ayah.
This is a particle that introduces and emphasizes what comes after it. It's like adding 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English.
Added هُ at the end which means 'he/it'. This connects back to the verb يُنَزِّلُ (he sends down) in the ayah, showing what 'he' does.
Added a small و mark (ۥ) after هُ which is just a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning
Basic negative particle that negates what comes after it. Here it negates إِلٰهَ (god) to form the first part of 'there is no god'
Added fatha mark to show clear pronunciation of first letter
Changed alif to alif madd (آ) to show long vowel sound
The root letters represent the basic concept of worship and divinity
Changed the first letter to إِ and added ا after second root letter to create a noun meaning the object of worship
Changed alif to alif maqsurah and added fatha at end. This word is being negated by لا in the phrase لا إله (there is no god)
Added shadda (ّ) to emphasize the meaning. This makes the exception stronger.
Added long alif (آ) at the end to show this connects to the next word أَنَا۠ (I). Together they mean 'except I' or 'only I'.
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'I'. It's one of the fundamental words in Arabic that doesn't come from a root.
The small circle (۠) is just a pronunciation mark showing this word should be read clearly and separately. It doesn't change the meaning.
Added اِتَّ at the beginning to show doing something to protect oneself. The ى at the end shows it's in the past.
Changed to a command for multiple people by adding وا at the end
Added فَ at beginning to connect this command with what came before it
Added نِ ('me') at the end to show Allah is telling people to fear Him directly, making it more personal and stronger

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is something that happened in the past. This word is connected to يُشْرِكُونَ (they associate partners) in this ayah, showing what Allah did versus what they do.
This is already in its basic past tense form with fatha marks
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun for the thing that is high up
Changed from single sky to multiple skies by adding ات and changing the middle of the word from ا to او
Added kasra at the end because this word is being acted upon by the verb خَلَقَ (He created)
Added 'the' (ال) at the start, which doubles the س sound
Adding the fatha vowel mark creates the basic noun. This word is what Allah created, along with ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens).

steps.m: the earth (object of خَلَقَ - He created)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite ('the earth') and fatḥa at the end shows it's the object
وَ means 'and', ٱلْ means 'the', and َ shows it's the object of the verb
The doubled letter makes it into a noun meaning 'truth' itself rather than the action of being truthful

steps.m: the truth (object of created in خَلَقَ)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it specific (the truth), and the kasra shows it follows the word بِ (with/by). This truth is what Allah created the heavens and earth with.

steps.m: with/by the truth (object of created in خَلَقَ)
steps.n: The بِ shows that truth was the means or way that Allah created the heavens and earth. This connects to خَلَقَ (He created) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: He is high above (عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ - what they associate with Him)
steps.n: Added تَ at start and ى at end to make it mean 'He is high/exalted' - a special form used for Allah's supreme status

steps.m: He is high above (عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ - what they associate with Him)
steps.n: Changed spelling to show long a sound with special alif
This is a basic preposition that shows separation or distance from something

steps.m: away from what (asking about what they associate with Allah)
steps.n: When عَنْ combines with ما (what), the ن changes to م and gets a shadda. This word is asking about what the disbelievers associate with Allah, showing disapproval of their actions.

steps.m: to make partners or join things together

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to those who associate partners with Allah.

steps.m: to create, form, or shape something

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word shows who created the نُّطْفَةٍ (drop of fluid) mentioned right after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to be close and friendly with others

steps.n: Changes the concept of being social into a noun for the social beings (humans)
Changes from humans as a group to referring to an individual person

steps.m: the human being (object of خَلَقَ/He created)
steps.n: Adds 'the' at the start and shows this is what was created. The word is the object of خَلَقَ (He created) in this ayah.
Basic preposition showing the material or source something comes from. In this ayah, it shows humans were created from/out of a fluid drop.
The kasra (ِ) under م shows this word connects to what comes after it
Changed from verb 'to drip' into noun 'a drop' by adding vowel marks
Added ة to show one specific instance
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning

steps.m: from a single drop of fluid (object of مِن)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this is the object of the preposition مِن (from)

steps.m: suddenly/then (introduces the result هُوَ خَصِيمٌ مُّبِينٌ)
steps.n: This is a special word that shows something happens unexpectedly. Here it introduces what happens after Allah creates humans from a tiny drop - suddenly they become argumentative!

steps.m: and then suddenly (introduces the result هُوَ خَصِيمٌ مُّبِينٌ)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this sudden event with what came before (Allah creating humans from a tiny drop)
Basic pronoun used to refer back to الإنسان (mankind) mentioned earlier in the ayah. Shows that mankind is the one being described as a clear opponent.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who disputes a lot (describing هُوَ)
steps.n: This form shows someone who frequently or intensely disputes. In this ayah, it's describing هُوَ (he/it)
The tanween shows this is indefinite, meaning 'a disputer' rather than 'the disputer'. This word describes هُوَ (he/it) in the ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone/something that does the action of making things clear
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning - showing something that really makes things clear
Added tanween to show this is describing something in a general way
Changed from root meaning 'comfort/benefit' to mean 'livestock' by using special pattern أَفْعَال which makes plural nouns
Added ال to specify these particular livestock

steps.m: and the livestock (object of خَلَقَهَا - He created them)
steps.n: Added و to connect to previous phrase and َ at end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb خَلَقَهَا (He created them)

steps.m: to create from nothing, to make something new
he created (referring to Allah creating الْأَنْعٰمَ/livestock)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. In this ayah, Allah is telling us about His creation of livestock animals.
Added هَا which refers back to الْأَنْعٰمَ (livestock) mentioned just before this word. هَا is used because الْأَنْعٰمَ is treated as feminine in Arabic.
Added fatha to the letter lam to show it's a preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'

steps.m: for you all (recipients of خَلَقَهَا - He created them)
steps.n: Added the pronoun كم (you all) to show who the preposition is directed to. This word connects to خَلَقَهَا (He created them) to show that the animals were created for you all.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment
The ها refers back to الْأَنْعٰمَ (livestock) which was mentioned in the ayah
Changed the verb into a noun that means the actual warmth you can feel, like from wool or fur of animals
Added the two dots on top at the end to show this is the subject form of the word
Changed from basic root meaning 'benefit' into a noun by adding vowels
Added مَ at the beginning to show where or what provides the benefit
Changed to broken plural form by adding alif after meem and making the middle part longer with a kasra sound
Added damma at the end to show this word is doing something in the sentence
Added و at beginning to connect to previous word, and wrote alif as ٰ (alif khanjariya)
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or its source. In this ayah, it's talking about what comes from cattle.
The و connects this to the previous benefits of cattle mentioned (warmth and benefits), adding another benefit
The ها refers back to الْأَنْعٰمَ (cattle) mentioned at the start of the ayah. This sets up what you eat from (تَأْكُلُونَ comes next).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Changes from past tense 'he ate' to present tense 'you eat' by adding تَ at start
Changes from singular to plural form
Adds the final vowel mark to complete the word
This is a basic preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'. It connects to فِيهَا (in them) in the next part of the ayah.
The suffix كُمْ means 'you all' - it's talking to multiple people about the benefits they get from animals mentioned in فِيهَا (in them)

steps.m: and for you all (connects to فِيهَا)
steps.n: The وَ at the start means 'and', connecting this to previous benefits of animals mentioned before this ayah
This is a basic preposition meaning 'in' or 'within'. Since it's a particle, we start with its basic form rather than a root.
Added هَا which refers back to الأنعام (livestock) mentioned earlier in the passage. This shows what the 'in' relates to.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding alif after the second root letter turns the verb into a noun meaning the quality itself
The double-damma shows this is the subject doing something in the sentence. Here it's the subject of فِيهَا (meaning 'in them/it')
This root relates to time and timing
Added vowel marks to make it into a noun meaning a specific time or moment
Added the fatha mark at the end to show this word is in the accusative case
This root is about rest and comfort
Changed the middle letter و to ا and added vowels to make 'he rested' in the past tense
Added أ at beginning to show causing something to happen
Changed from 'he' to 'she' by adding تُ at start

steps.m: you all bring to rest (your cattle)
steps.n: Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing this. In this ayah, it refers to bringing cattle to rest in the evening.
The ending shows this word is being used to tell when something happens
The kasra (ِ) vowel on the first letter makes this a noun meaning 'time'
The fatha (َ) at the end shows this word is being used as a direct object

steps.m: and at the time (when تُرِيحُونَ - you bring back)
steps.n: The و connects this to the previous phrase about beauty in the cattle, showing they have beauty at this specific time
This root is about letting animals graze freely
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Removed first vowel and changed ending to make it present tense 'let graze'
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added ونَ at the end to show multiple people are doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
ت at start shows it's either 'she' or 'you' doing the action
Added و with fatha to connect this clause to the previous statement
Added vowels (fatha-damma-fatha) to make it mean 'it became heavy' in the past
Changed vowel pattern to create a noun meaning 'weight' or 'burden'
Changed from single weight to plural form by adding 'a' at start and 'aal' at end
Added suffix meaning 'your (plural)'. This word is the object (thing being carried) in relation to وَتَحْمِلُ (and they carry)
This is a basic preposition showing direction or movement towards something (like 'to' in English). It connects the word بَلَدٍ (city/town) to what comes before it.
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى which helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning. This preposition connects to the word بَلَدٍ (city/town) that comes after it.

steps.m: land/city (that you carry your loads to)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a noun. This word refers to the destination mentioned in إِلَىٰ (to) from the previous word.

steps.m: a land/city (that you could not reach)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this is any land/city, and it's being described by the words that follow it (لَّمْ تَكُونُوا۟ بٰلِغِيهِ - which you could not reach).
Basic negative particle that makes verbs refer to the past and changes their ending

steps.m: not (in the past, connected to تَكُونُوا۟)
steps.n: Added emphasis by doubling the ل letter. This word connects to تَكُونُوا۟ (you were) to say 'you were not'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
تَ at start shows it's about 'she' and the damma marks show it's happening now
Added وا at the end to show this refers to a group of people
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to active participle form showing who does the reaching
Added 'my' by putting ي at the end
Added object pronoun ه (it/him) and changed alif to alif with bar. This word is the object that you were not able to reach in the phrase لَّمْ تَكُونُوا۟ بٰلِغِيهِ (you were not reaching it)
This is a frozen form particle used for exception. It is not derived from a root and cannot be broken down morphologically.
Changed the vowel to kasra and doubled the middle letter to turn the action into a noun meaning the difficulty itself

steps.m: with hardship (describes how أَثْقَالَكُمْ/your loads are carried)
steps.n: Added preposition ب to show how the loads are carried - with difficulty. This connects to the earlier word تَحْمِلُ (carries) in the verse.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the singular noun
Forms broken plural pattern أَفْعُل for small quantities (3-10)
Adding ال makes it specific/definite
Adding kasra at the end shows this word follows another word in the sentence
This is a basic emphasis word that strengthens what comes after it, like adding 'truly' or 'certainly' in English.

The shadda (ّ) makes the 'n' sound stronger to show extra emphasis. The kasra (ِ) under the first letter shows this word affects what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes that 'your Lord is truly Kind and Merciful.'
The root ر-ب-ب becomes رَبَّ (Lord/Master) with a doubled ب to show strong care and nurturing. This word connects to لَرَءُوفٌ (most kind) later in the verse.
Adding كُمْ shows this nurturing lord belongs to or cares for multiple people ('your' for a group)

steps.m: extremely kind and merciful (one who does this)
steps.n: Changed the root into a special form that shows someone who has this quality very strongly
Adds emphatic لَ prefix to emphasize the meaning

steps.m: surely extremely kind and merciful (describing Allah)
steps.n: The ٌ (tanween) at the end shows this is a complete quality/description, like saying 'truly merciful' rather than 'the merciful one'
Added vowels and ي to show someone who has this quality strongly
Added shadda (ّ) for emphasis and tanween (ٌ) to show this is describing Allah's attribute mentioned in the ayah
Basic noun pattern for collective nouns
Adding ال makes it refer to specific horses rather than horses in general
Added و at beginning to connect it with previous items, and fatha at end to show it's the object of an implied verb 'He created'
Adds vowels to make it a proper noun meaning 'mule'
Changes from singular 'mule' to plural 'mules' by adding alif
Added the definite article ال
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb
Added و at beginning to connect with previous word وَٱلْخَيْلَ (and the horses)
This pattern فِعال is commonly used for animal names
This is a common way to make plurals for animal names
Makes the noun definite by adding the definite article
Adds conjunction 'and' to connect with previous items (horses and mules) mentioned in the verse

steps.m: and the donkeys (as objects of لِتَرْكَبُوهَا - for you to ride them)
steps.n: Adds the accusative case ending to show this is the object of the verb 'to ride' mentioned later in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense form by adding ta- prefix and adjusting vowels
Added waw with damma to indicate plural masculine subject

steps.m: you all ride/mount them (referring to الْخَيْلَ وَٱلْبِغَالَ وَٱلْحَمِيرَ - the horses, mules and donkeys)
steps.n: Added هَا (meaning 'them') at the end to show what is being ridden (the animals mentioned before)

steps.m: so that you all may ride/mount them
steps.n: Added 'li' at the start to mean 'so that' or 'in order to' - shows the purpose of riding them
Added kasra under first letter to change from action to concept

steps.m: a specific beautiful thing or decoration
steps.n: Changes from general 'beauty' to a specific beautiful object or decoration you can see

steps.m: decoration/adornment (object of the verb خَلَقَ - He created)
steps.n: The ً (tanween) at the end shows this is a direct object - something that was created, not just any decoration

steps.m: and decoration/adornment (connected to الْخَيْلَ وَالْبِغَالَ وَالْحَمِيرَ)
steps.n: Connects this to the list of other things Allah created (horses, mules and donkeys) that were mentioned before
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Present tense form showing ongoing creation

steps.m: and he creates (connected to لِتَرْكَبُوهَا - for you to ride them)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect this with the previous statement about animals being created for riding

This is a relative pronoun used to refer to things. In this ayah, it refers to other things that Allah creates beyond what was mentioned before (horses, mules, and donkeys). The word connects to يَخْلُقُ (He creates) to mean 'what He creates'.
This is a negative particle that makes something negative, like adding 'not' in English. Here it negates the verb تَعْلَمُونَ (you know) to mean 'you do not know'
Added a fatha mark (َ) on the ل to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'laa'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The ت at the start and the vowel changes show this is happening now and talking to someone
Adding ون at the end makes it plural - now talking to many people. This verb is negated by لا (not) in the ayah.
Form used for feminine superlative (meaning 'highest')

steps.m: and on/upon (connects to what came before)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Added ال to make it specific and unique, then merged letters according to Arabic rules. This makes it a proper name for The One God.
Added shadda on ل, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added kasra at end to show possession. This word owns or possesses قَصْدُ (the directing) in this ayah.

steps.m: to aim straight towards a goal

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make the root letters into a noun meaning 'right direction'. This word shows what belongs to Allah in this ayah.
Added the ُ (damma) ending to show this word is doing the action in the sentence
Adding ي and vowels changes the word from a basic root into a noun meaning 'path'
Added ال to make it specific. The ل in ال combines with س creating a doubled sound (shown by ّ)
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes before it in the sentence
This is a basic preposition showing source or origin

steps.m: from it/her (referring back to السَّبِيلِ - the path)
steps.n: The word مِن (from) gets هَا (meaning 'it/her') attached to its end to show what we're talking from - in this case the path (السَّبِيلِ)

steps.m: and from it/her (referring back to السَّبِيلِ - the path)
steps.n: The و connects this to the previous phrase

steps.m: to turn away or deviate from the right path
one who turns away (from the straight path)
steps.n: Added ا after ج to show who does the action. The و changed to ا because it's a weak letter.

steps.m: one who turns away (from the straight path)
steps.n: The alif is written with a special mark (آ) when it comes after a fatha. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: one who turns away (describing the wrong path)
steps.n: Added tanween damma to show this describes something in general. In this ayah, it's describing the wrong or crooked path, as opposed to the straight path mentioned earlier.
This word starts an 'if' statement about something that didn't actually occur

steps.m: and if (hypothetical - didn't happen) (connects to شَآءَ)
steps.n: The و connects this conditional statement to what came before. This starts a hypothetical statement 'and if He had willed' that continues with شَآءَ (He willed)
Added fatha vowels to connect the letters and show this is a past tense verb
The weak letter ي changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs

steps.m: to guide or show someone the right way

steps.n: Added fatha marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. The ي at the end changes to ى because it's at the end of the word.
Added كُمْ at the end to show who was guided (you all)

steps.m: he would surely have guided you all (result of لَوْ شَآءَ - if He had willed)

steps.m: and rivers (as direct object of أَلْقَىٰ/placed)

This is a pronoun that doesn't come from a root. It refers to a group of people who are the subject (doers) of the verb يَهْتَدُونَ (they are guided).

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: مَن refers to someone or something being discussed. Here it refers to the one who creates, as shown by the verb يَخْلُقُ (creates) that comes after it.

steps.m: he creates (contrasted with لَّا يَخْلُقُ - does not create)

steps.m: like the one who (comparing to يَخْلُقُ - creates)
steps.n: Added كَ at the beginning to make a comparison. In this ayah, it's comparing someone who creates with someone who doesn't create.

The shadda (doubled ل) makes the negation stronger. This word is negating the verb يَخْلُقُ (creates) to say 'does not create'
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters

steps.m: he creates (comparing with لَّا يَخْلُقُ - does not create)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by: 1) adding يَ at start to show 'he' is doing it now, 2) putting sukun on first root letter, 3) putting damma on second root letter and end to show it's happening now. This word is being compared with لَّا يَخْلُقُ (does not create) in this ayah.

steps.m: is it true that? (questioning what follows)
steps.n: This particle turns a statement into a question, making listeners think about what comes next

steps.m: then will you not (think about تَذَكَّرُونَ)

Added ون ending to change from 'he' to 'you all' doing the action

steps.m: if (introduces the condition: 'if you try to count Allah's blessings')
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical situation. In this ayah, it introduces the condition of trying to count Allah's blessings.

steps.m: and if (connects to previous statement and introduces condition: 'if you try to count Allah's blessings')
steps.n: The و adds 'and' to connect this conditional statement with what came before. The full condition it introduces is about counting Allah's blessings, which the next words tell us is impossible to do completely.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he counted' to 'count!' by removing vowels and adding a shadda, since the word has two 'd' letters next to each other

steps.m: you all count (plural command to count نِعْمَةَ ٱللَّهِ - Allah's favors)

Changed from a verb (to bless) into a noun (one blessing) by changing vowels and adding ة at the end
Added fatha at the end because this blessing is being counted (object of تَعُدُّوا)
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'

steps.m: to count, calculate, or keep track of

Adding تُ at the start changes it from a command to 'you' doing the action

This is the basic form of the word that shows a condition might happen
Adding the shadda makes the meaning stronger and more certain. This word introduces اللَّهَ (Allah) in this ayah to emphasize His attributes.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being counted in نِعْمَةَ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a shadda (double L sound), a special alif at start, and a fatha mark to show it's connected to the word 'blessings' before it

steps.m: to forgive, pardon, or cover faults

Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling mercy

Added tanween dammah (double damma) at the end to make the word indefinite
The ر has a shaddah (doubled) because it comes after the word Allah, not because of the tanween which was already there in both forms

The ُ (damma) at the end shows that Allah is the one doing the action in this sentence - specifically, Allah is the one who knows (يَعْلَمُ).

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of يَعْلَمُ)

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: and what (that which you reveal in تُعْلِنُونَ)

steps.m: the arrogant one (object of يُحِبُّ - loves)

steps.m: to them (being asked about what was sent down)
steps.n: Added هُم which means 'them' when referring to a group of males or mixed group. In this ayah, it refers to the people being asked about what their Lord has sent down.

steps.n: The root letters change to make a noun meaning 'the act of standing/rising'
Adding ة makes it refer to the specific event of rising from death
Adding ال makes it refer to THE resurrection - the specific Day of Judgment
Added kasra at end because it follows يَوْمَ (day). The alif is written as ٰ for style.
This is the base particle meaning 'from'. It shows where something starts or comes from.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. In this ayah, it connects to the previous phrase about carrying burdens.
Changed from root meaning 'carry weight' to noun meaning 'burden' by adding vowel marks
Changed to plural form by adding أَ at start and ا after second root letter
Added kasra at end to show possession, connecting it to هُمْ (their) in أَوْزَارَهُمْ
Starting with the basic demonstrative ذا and changing it to the relative pronoun form
Added ين to make it plural, showing it refers to multiple people

steps.m: those who (subject of يُضِلُّونَهُم - they lead astray)
steps.n: Added ال at beginning to make it definite and specific. The shadda on ل shows it's emphasized. This word is the subject (doer) of يُضِلُّونَهُم (they lead astray) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters and added shadda to show emphasis
Changes from past to present tense by adding يُ at the start
Added masculine plural subject marker ون

steps.m: they lead them astray (object of يَحْمِلُوا)
steps.n: The هُم (them) was added to show who is being led astray - it connects to 'they bear' later in the verse

steps.m: to change, alter, or make different

steps.n: Changes from root meaning into a noun meaning 'other than'
Added the preposition بِ which connects this word to عِلْمٍ (knowledge) in the ayah
Added kasra at the end because it comes before the word عِلْمٍ (knowledge) in the ayah
Changed the action of knowing into the thing itself (knowledge) by adding the vowels ِ and ْ
Added ٍ at the end to show this word means 'any knowledge' and is being used after بِغَيْرِ (without) in the phrase 'without any knowledge'
This is a special attention word that doesn't come from a root. It's used to make people pay attention to what comes next.
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce this attention-getting word. It's like saying 'Hey, listen!' before saying something important.
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'it became bad'
Changed to common spelling with alif due to weak middle letter
The ا is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's a long vowel. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.
This is a special word used to ask 'what' or refer to 'what/that which'. It's a particle, so it doesn't come from a root. In this ayah, it's asking about what they bear/carry.
Added fatha mark on م to show the correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The و drops because it's a weak letter at the start after adding يَ.
Added ونَ to show multiple people (they) are doing the action
The words are the same - both mean 'they bear/carry'. The only difference is the diacritical mark (damma) is shown in the first one but hidden in the second one
This is a special word that adds certainty when used with past tense verbs (like مَكَرَ here) and shows likelihood with present tense verbs. Here it emphasizes that the plotting definitely happened.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word comes after قَدْ which emphasizes that this plotting definitely happened.
Added ال with shadda to make it definite. This word connects what comes before with what comes after, like saying 'the one who' in English.
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb مَكَرَ (plotted/schemed) in this ayah.
Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from
This root carries the basic meaning of something coming before something else in time or space
Root letters ق-ب-ل got vowel marks (َ and ْ) to make the word 'before'
Added kasra to show this connects to the word that follows it
Added هِمْ to show this means 'before them'
Added vowel marks and the ى at the end because it's a special word ending in ي. This word shows what happened before the ceiling fell (فَخَرَّ).

steps.m: so he came (followed by فَخَرَّ/it fell)
steps.n: The فَ shows this action led to the ceiling falling (فَخَرَّ) in the next part of the ayah
Changed the root into the basic noun form meaning 'a deity'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Changes from basic root to noun meaning a structure
Added هُم with fatha before it to show possession. This building is what Allah came to (فَأَتَى) in the verse.
Basic preposition meaning 'from' - used to show where something starts or comes from
The 'n' sound gets doubled (gets a shadda) because it comes after stopping in speech, making the word stronger
Added fatha at the end to connect it with the next word القَوَاعِدِ (the foundations)

steps.m: to sit firmly or be established

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding vowels
Changed pattern to show someone/something that does the action
Changed to plural form that means 'foundations' - things that sit firmly and support other things
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ِ to show it follows the word مِنَ (from)

steps.m: to fall, collapse, or tumble down
it fell down heavily (subject of building in previous ayah)
steps.n: The doubled ر (with shadda) shows this wasn't just a simple fall - it was a heavy collapse. This refers to the building mentioned in the previous ayah.

steps.m: and so it collapsed (as a result of Allah destroying its foundations)
steps.n: The فَ shows this collapse happened as a direct result of Allah destroying the foundations mentioned in the previous ayah
This is the basic preposition meaning 'on' or 'upon'
The ى changes to يْ when connecting to pronouns
Added هِمُ at the end to show 'them'. This word connects to السَّقْفُ (the roof) which falls upon them in this ayah.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun for the thing that covers from above
Added ٱل to make it specific and ُ to show it's doing the falling action in the verse. The roof fell (خَرَّ) upon them.
This is a preposition that shows something comes from somewhere. In this ayah, it connects the word قَبْلِ (before) with هِمْ (them) to mean 'before them'.
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make a word for position or location
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by what comes after it
Added the pronoun هِمْ (them) to show who it's above

steps.m: he came (subject of punishment in this ayah)
steps.n: Added superscript alif (ٰ) after ى to show it's a long vowel
Added هُمُ to show who received the action (them). The مُ is added to help pronunciation when connecting words.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous phrase about the roof falling on them
Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun meaning the result of causing pain/suffering
Adding ال at the beginning makes it specific, like saying 'the punishment' instead of just any punishment
The ُ at the end shows this is doing the action of 'coming' in the phrase 'the punishment came to them'
This is a preposition showing something comes from somewhere. In this ayah, it connects 'fell' (خَرَّ) with 'above them' (فَوْقِهِمْ) to show the roof fell from above them.
This word is used to talk about a place or location
The word means 'where/from where' and shows location. The vowel marks حَيْثُ help us read it correctly. In this ayah, it connects to لَا يَشْعُرُونَ to mean 'from where they did not perceive'
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَشْعُرُونَ (they perceive) negative, changing its meaning to 'they do not perceive'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed from past to present by adding يَ at start and changing vowels - like changing 'felt' to 'feels'

steps.m: they sense/feel/are aware (in the present) (subject of لا)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This word comes after لا (not) in the ayah, so it means 'they do not sense'

steps.m: then (connects this part to what came before)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce this connecting word. This word shows that what follows comes after what came before in time or importance.

steps.m: on the day (of ٱلْقِيٰمَةِ - the Resurrection)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this word is telling us when something happens. It connects directly to ٱلْقِيٰمَةِ (the Resurrection) to tell us which day.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changes verb 'stood' into noun meaning 'act of standing'
Adds ة to specify this particular type of rising - the resurrection
Makes the word definite by adding the definite article
Shows this word is connected to what comes after it in the sentence
Changed from root letters to past tense verb meaning 'he was disgraced'
Changes from 'was disgraced' to 'makes someone disgraced' - like changing from feeling shame to making others feel shame
Adds 'them' as the object - shows who is being disgraced
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense form. The middle و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle ا changes back to و in present tense.

steps.m: and he says (introducing Allah's question أَيْنَ شُرَكَآءِىَ)
steps.n: Adding وَ at the start connects this to the previous statement. This introduces Allah's question to the disbelievers about their false partners.

steps.m: where (asking about the location of شُرَكَآءِىَ/my partners)
steps.n: This is a question word that asks about location. In this ayah, Allah is asking about the location of the false partners/gods that people claimed.

steps.m: to partner with or join together

steps.n: Changes from a verb (to partner) into a noun (a partner)
Changes single partner into multiple partners
Added kasra at end to show this word is possessed by something
Added ىَ at end to show these are 'my' partners
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun used to connect descriptions to nouns
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people. This word is connected to كُنتُمْ (you all were) in the next part of the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
The word changes from past tense 'he was' to a command 'be!' by dropping the middle letter و and changing vowels

steps.m: you all were (addressing multiple people in تُشٰٓقُّونَ)
steps.n: Added تُمْ to show this refers to multiple people being addressed. This matches with تُشٰٓقُّونَ (you all opposed) in the ayah.

steps.m: to split apart or be in opposition

steps.n: Added alif after first letter and shadda on last letter to show strong mutual opposition
Added تُ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action

steps.m: you all oppose/disagree with (intensely) (subject of كُنتُمْ)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This word is connected to كُنتُمْ (you were) in the ayah.
This combines the preposition في (in) with هم (them)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels. This word is related to يَقُولُ (he says) later in the ayah, showing a sequence of speech.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in words with و in the middle.
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ذ letter to connect them smoothly. This word connects descriptions to people or things, like saying 'the one who' in English.
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word is connected to كُنتُمْ (you all were) in this ayah.
Changed to passive form by adding damma at start and kasra in middle. The weak letter ي changes to ي with fatha.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people. The ي changes to و because of the plural. This refers to those who the previous verse was talking about.
Added kasra on first letter and sukun on second letter to change from action (knowing) to thing (knowledge)
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific knowledge rather than just any knowledge

steps.m: the knowledge (object of أُوتُوا - were given)
steps.n: Changed alif to hamzat wasl and added fatha at end to show this is the object (thing being given) in the phrase 'those who were given knowledge'
Added kasra (ِ) under the first letter to help with pronunciation

steps.m: indeed/truly (emphasizes خِزْىَ 'disgrace' and سُوٓءَ 'evil' that follow)
steps.n: Added shadda (ّ) to strengthen the word's emphasis. This word introduces and emphasizes the 'disgrace' and 'evil' mentioned right after it in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to change the verb into a noun meaning the state of being disgraced

steps.m: disgrace (object of يُخْزِيهِمْ - he disgraces them)
steps.n: Changed the final ي to ى and added َ to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: the disgrace (object of يُخْزِيهِمْ - he disgraces them)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ (al) at the beginning to make it specific - referring to a particular disgrace
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun. The و keeps a sukun because it's a weak letter.
Added ال to make it specific/definite - now we're talking about a particular day
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being used as an object in the sentence
Connected the root letters and added damma to change from action (being bad) to thing (evil/badness)
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda because it comes after ال
Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of Allah humiliating them (يُخْزِيهِمْ)
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it
This is a preposition that shows one thing affecting or being above another. Here it shows that something (disgrace and evil) is falling upon or affecting the disbelievers.
Basic past tense form showing someone did this action
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changes from showing an action to showing who does the action
Makes the word plural to show multiple people who reject truth
Adds 'the' at the beginning and shows this word is affected by the word عَلَى (upon) in the verse
This is a relative pronoun used to describe a group of people. It connects two parts of a sentence.
Added shadda to middle letter to show intensity of action
Added ت at beginning to show the action is being done to or for oneself
Added تَ at beginning to show feminine subject (referring to المَلائِكَةُ - the angels)
Added alif maqsurah at end due to weak letter rule

steps.m: she receives/takes them completely (subject: الْمَلٰٓئِكَةُ - the angels)
steps.n: Added هُمُ to show who is being taken (them)
Root letters م-ل-ك mean 'power/authority', adding fathas creates مَلَك meaning 'angel' - one who has divine authority
The singular 'angel' becomes plural by adding alif and changing the middle of the word
Added ة to show this is a collective group
Added ال to make it definite and ُ to show it's the subject, with proper spelling of alif
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who wrongs others (object of تَتَوَفَّى - take in death)
steps.n: This form creates a noun describing someone who does the action. The word is in accusative case because it is the object of the verb تَتَوَفَّى (take/receive in death).
The ي at the end makes this a plural form, showing multiple wrongdoers
The ى at the end shows this word is being possessed by something

steps.m: to breathe, to be precious, self

steps.n: The root letters are given vowels to make a noun meaning 'self' or 'soul'. When combined with 'their', it becomes 'their selves'.
Forms broken plural by adding initial hamza and changing internal vowel pattern

steps.m: their selves (referring to the wrongdoers mentioned in ظَالِمِىٓ)
steps.n: Adding هم shows possession by the wrongdoers mentioned earlier in the verse
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Form IV verb (added أ) that means 'to throw/cast something'. Shows someone actively throwing or offering peace, rather than just meeting it.
Adds conjunction meaning 'and/so/then'
Adds plural masculine subject marker indicating 'they'

steps.m: to be at peace, to be safe

steps.n: Adding vowel marks creates the basic past tense verb form
Adding shadda emphasizes the completeness of the peace/submission
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific peace/submission
Adding fatha at the end shows this word is the object of the verb فَأَلْقَوُا۟ (then they offered)

steps.m: not (what) (negates كُنَّا نَعْمَلُ - we used to do)
steps.n: This is a negative particle that denies what comes after it. In this ayah, it's used with كُنَّا نَعْمَلُ (we used to do) to mean 'we did not do'
Added vowels (a-a-a) to make it 'he was' in the past tense
The middle weak letter و drops out and vowels adjust according to Arabic rules for hollow verbs
Added emphasis by doubling the ن
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: we do/work (in response to مَا كُنَّا - 'we were not')
steps.n: The ن at the beginning shows 'we' are doing the action, while the sukun and damma marks change it to present tense. This is part of the disbelievers' false claim that 'we were not doing any evil.'

This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or is made of something. Here it connects what they were doing (نَعْمَلُ) with evil (سُوٓءٍۭ), showing they claim they weren't doing any evil.
This is the basic concept of being bad or evil
Changed the action of being evil into a noun meaning evil itself
Added a long vowel mark over the و to show proper pronunciation
Added kasra tanween to show this is receiving the action of the word 'from' (مِن) in the phrase 'we weren't doing any evil'

steps.m: yes indeed (responding to the previous negative statement)
steps.n: This is a special word used to strongly contradict a negative statement. In this ayah, it's responding to their claim 'we weren't doing any evil' by affirming that they were indeed doing evil.

steps.m: yes indeed (responding to the previous negative statement)
steps.n: The alif at the end is written with a special mark (ٰ) called an alif maqsurah, which is just a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: yes indeed (responding to the previous negative statement)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ٓ) above the word to show that we pause slightly when reading it

Adding the shadda (double ن) changes this from a conditional word to an emphasis word. It makes what comes after it stronger, like adding an exclamation mark in English. Here it emphasizes that Allah is truly All-Knowing.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows the ل is doubled.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being referred to)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is being acted upon or referred to in the sentence. Also changed initial alif to wasl form.
Added ي between second and third root letters and vowels to show someone who possesses this quality intensely
This word describes Allah's attribute of complete and perfect knowledge. It stays in nominative case (with ٌ) because it's the predicate of إنَّ.
Basic preposition indicating connection or means
Added kasra to help pronunciation

steps.m: with/in what (connects to تَعْمَلُونَ - you used to do)
steps.n: Added ما which means 'what' to show this refers to the actions they used to do. This word connects to تَعْمَلُونَ (you used to do) in the ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
The middle weak letter و drops out, and vowels change to match the pattern
Added تُ at the end to show who did the action in the past

steps.m: you all were (addressing the disbelievers in تَعْمَلُونَ)
steps.n: Added م with sukoon to show this is addressing multiple people. This matches with تَعْمَلُونَ (you all do) in the ayah, showing both words are talking to the same group.

steps.m: to do, to act, to work

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense

steps.m: you (all) do/work (in response to كُنتُمْ - you were)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are being addressed. This verb describes what the disbelievers used to do, as referenced by كُنتُمْ (you were) earlier in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fathas to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he entered' to 'enter!' by adding hamza at start and changing vowels to make it a command
Added وا to make it a command to multiple people
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from something before. The alif changes to hamzat-wasl (ٱ) because it comes after فَ.
Adding alif makes it a common noun pattern for objects/places
Added hamza prefix and changed internal vowels to make plural. This word is what is being entered in the phrase 'enter the doors'
Changed the middle alif to a long alif (with a small line on top) - both words mean 'doors' and show what is being entered

steps.m: Hell (the destination being entered through أَبْوٰبَ/gates)
steps.n: This is a proper noun that doesn't come from a root. It's a name for the place of punishment in the afterlife

steps.m: Hell (the destination being entered through أَبْوٰبَ/gates)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word - fatha after ج and ه, shadda on ن, and fatha after م. The shadda shows we say the ن twice.

steps.m: to last or continue without end

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changes the verb into a word describing someone who does this action

steps.m: those remaining forever (describes the people entering hell)
steps.n: Changes from describing one person/thing to many people, and shows they will stay forever (plural form of lasting/remaining)
This is a preposition that shows being inside something. Here it specifically refers to being inside Hell (جَهَنَّمَ) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added ها which refers back to Hell (جَهَنَّمَ). The ها is feminine because جَهَنَّمَ is treated as feminine in Arabic.
Changes from basic root to past tense verb meaning 'how bad!'
Adds 'so/then' to connect this phrase to what came before

steps.m: dwelling place (of ٱلْمُتَكَبِّرِينَ - the arrogant ones)
steps.n: Added مَ at the beginning to show place, added ى at the end to make it a noun. This word describes the residence or dwelling place of the arrogant ones (ٱلْمُتَكَبِّرِينَ) in Hell.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the action more intense or shows making something have this quality
Adding تَ shows doing the action to oneself
Adding مُ at start and changing vowels shows who does the action
Adding ين makes it plural and shows it's describing something (in this case, it describes مَثْوَى - the dwelling)
Adding ال (the) makes it 'the arrogant ones' instead of just 'arrogant ones' - describes their dwelling place
Added vowels to connect the letters. The middle letter و changes to ا when we add the vowels.
Changed from active (he said) to passive (it was said) by changing vowels and middle letter

steps.m: and it was said (connecting to what came before in the ayah)
steps.n: The و connects this phrase to the previous part of the ayah
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation. The shadda on ذ shows it's emphasized.
Added ين at the end to make it plural, referring to multiple people instead of just one person
Added لِ at beginning to mean 'for'. The ل combines with ال, shown by the shadda (ّ) on the ل

steps.m: to protect or guard against harm

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make a basic past tense verb
Added اِتَّ at the beginning to show doing the action to oneself. The ت combines with ق using shadda (ّ) for easier pronunciation.
Added وْا at the end to show many people did this action in the past
Added ٱ to help pronounce the word since it starts with two consonants
This is the basic question word used to ask about things. In this ayah, it's used to ask 'What has your Lord sent down?'

steps.m: what (asking about أَنزَلَ - what was sent down)
steps.n: ذَا is added to make the question more specific and emphatic. It's like saying 'what exactly' instead of just 'what'

steps.m: what (asking about أَنزَلَ - what was sent down)
steps.n: The alif is elongated to مَاذَآ for emphasis in recitation, but doesn't change the meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added hamza with fatha (أَ) at start and sukun on ن to show making someone else do the action
Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to show complete mastery and lordship
Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence

steps.m: your Lord (in response to مَاذَآ أَنزَلَ)
steps.n: Added suffix كُمْ to show possession by multiple people being addressed. This word appears in response to the question 'What did... send down?'
This is the basic concept of speaking or saying something
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter

steps.m: they said (answering the question مَاذَآ أَنزَلَ رَبُّكُمْ - what has your Lord sent down?)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people (they) did the action. This word is their response to the question 'what has your Lord sent down?'
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and form the basic noun meaning 'good'
Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this is the object of what they said. When someone asks what Allah revealed, they responded with this word.
This is the basic relative pronoun with proper voweling. It points to one person or thing.
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people (masculine plural)
Adding لِ shows purpose or direction - 'for' or 'to'
The ل gets a shadda because two ل letters come together (the one from لِ and the one from ال)
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning and changed vowels to make it mean 'to make something good' or 'to do good'
Added وا at the end to show that a group of people did the action

This is a preposition that shows something is inside or contained within something else. Here it connects to 'this worldly life' (هٰذِهِ ٱلدُّنْيَا) to show what contains the good things being discussed.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this' for masculine objects that are close by
Changed the ending to make it feminine. This word points to something close by that is feminine, like 'this life' (هٰذِهِ الدُّنْيَا) in this ayah

steps.m: to be near or close to
steps.n: This is the basic concept of nearness or closeness
Root د-ن-و means 'near' - added vowels and ي to make دُنْيا meaning 'worldly life' or 'this world'
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the د letter because it comes after ال. The word came to refer specifically to this worldly life because it's closer/lower compared to the afterlife.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic adjective form
Adding ة changes it from a general adjective to a specific feminine noun meaning 'a good deed'
Adding tanween (double dammah) makes it indefinite, meaning 'a' good deed
Forming noun meaning 'house/abode'
The root letters د-و-ر became دَار (house) by adding vowels. The و changed to ا, which is common in Arabic when making nouns from roots with و.
Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added لَ to emphasize the statement, like saying 'surely' or 'indeed'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it

steps.m: to come later or be behind
steps.n: Forming active participle
Added alif after first letter and vowels to show who/what does the action. This creates the doer pattern that we'll use to describe the next life.
Added ة to make it feminine, which helps specify this refers to the next life specifically
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end to show it's owned by the word دَارُ (home/abode) in the phrase دَارُ ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ (home of the Hereafter)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and form the basic word meaning 'good' or 'better'
Added double-damma at the end to show this is the subject doing the action. In this ayah, it's describing what Allah sent down as being better/best.
This is a special word used to praise something. Added vowel marks to show it's being used as a praise word.
Added ل at start to make the praise stronger, like saying 'truly' or 'indeed'
Added وَ (and) to connect this praise with the previous statement about the home of the hereafter

steps.m: to move in a circle or around something
steps.n: Forming noun meaning 'house/abode'
Adding the vowel mark to make it a noun meaning a place
Added damma at the end because this word is the subject being described as 'better' in the phrase وَلَدَارُ ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ خَيْرٌ (and the home of the Hereafter is better)

steps.m: to protect or guard against harm
steps.n: These are the basic letters that give us the core meaning of protection
Root letters got vowels (َ) to make 'he protected' in past tense
Added مُتَ at the beginning to show someone protecting themselves (reflexive action)
The و combines with ت to make تّ (with shadda) for easier pronunciation
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to point to specific God-fearing people and َ at the end because it's being acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: to cover or conceal something completely

steps.n: Adding vowel marks changes it from a root into a real word

The root جَنَنَ (cover/conceal) becomes جَنَّة (garden) first, then ات is added to make it plural gardens. Gardens are called this because their trees cover/conceal what's beneath. Links to عَدْنٍ to mean 'Gardens of Eden'

steps.m: gardens (as the main subject that يَدْخُلُونَهَا 'they will enter')
steps.n: The ُ shows these gardens are the main subject that the next verb يَدْخُلُونَهَا (they will enter) is talking about
This is a special word that doesn't come from a root - it means a place of permanent stay
The tanween kasra (ٍ) shows this word is describing the gardens (جَنَّاتُ) that come before it, making it mean 'gardens of eternal dwelling' or 'gardens of paradise'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding ي at start and changing vowel patterns

steps.m: they enter (referring to the righteous ones)
steps.n: This plural form refers back to المتقين (the righteous ones) mentioned at the end of the ayah

steps.m: they enter it (referring to جَنَّاتُ - the gardens)
steps.n: هَا refers to جَنَّاتُ (gardens) mentioned at the start of the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

steps.m: it (she) flows (referring to rivers flowing under جَنَّاتُ)
steps.n: Added تَ at start to show present tense feminine subject, changed vowels to show ongoing action. This describes the rivers (feminine) flowing under the gardens mentioned.
This is a connecting word (particle) that shows where something starts from. In this ayah, it connects 'flows' (تَجْرِى) with 'beneath it' (تَحْتِهَا) to show rivers flowing from beneath.
Adding vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'under'
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something else
Added هَا to show this is beneath the gardens mentioned earlier. This whole phrase تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا means 'flowing from beneath them (the gardens)'.
Root letters got vowels (fat-ha) to form the simple noun meaning 'river'
Changed to plural form by adding أ at start and ا after second root letter
Added ال to make it definite ('the rivers')
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the flowing (تَجْرِى) in this ayah
This is a basic particle that shows direction or purpose

steps.m: for them (referring to the believers who will enter جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who the action is for. This connects to the previous mention of the believers who will enter gardens of Eden (جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ)
Basic preposition showing location or containment within something. In this ayah, it shows location within جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ (Gardens of Eden)
The ها refers back to جَنَّاتُ (Gardens) which is feminine in Arabic

steps.m: whatever (things they want in جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ)
steps.n: This word is connecting to يَشَآءُونَ (they wish/want) to show that they can have whatever they want in the gardens. In this context, it's not asking a question but showing unlimited possibilities.
The root letters connect and the middle letter ي becomes ا to make the past tense 'he wanted'
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed end vowel to ُ to match

steps.m: they want/wish (referring to the believers in Paradise)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action. The long alif (آ) is written to show the extended sound.
The basic demonstrative word 'this' is extended with alif to mean 'that' (pointing to something farther away)
Added لِ which helps connect the word parts together

steps.m: like that (describes how يَجْزِى/rewards will be given)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end (not beginning) to make it mean 'that'. Points to how the rewards will be given, like when we point at something to show it.

steps.m: to give what is due (reward or punishment)

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense verb meaning 'he rewarded/punished'

steps.m: he gives what is due (subject is اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This shows Allah is the one who rewards.

steps.m: he gives what is due (with weak letter ending)
steps.n: Changed the ending to ى because this is a special type of verb that ends with a weak letter instead of regular ي
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' or 'something worshipped'
Adding 'the' makes it refer to a specific deity rather than any deity
Adding the u-mark shows this word is the subject of the sentence
Added fathas to make basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and doubled ت (with shadda) to change from 'protected' to 'someone who protects themselves from bad things'

steps.m: the one who guards themselves (against evil)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite

steps.m: the ones who guard themselves (against evil)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to make it plural - means 'many people who guard against evil' instead of just one person
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific. The ل gets a shadda because it combines with the following letter.
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word is the subject of تَتَوَفَّىٰهُمُ (take their souls) in this ayah.
Added shadda to show doing something completely or thoroughly
Added تَ at the start to show doing something to yourself or receiving something (like angels receiving souls)
Added ه at the end to show the action is done to someone (him)
Added تَ at start and changed ending to show angels are taking their souls (present tense)
Changed the root 'to have power' into a noun meaning 'angel' by adding the vowels a-a
Changed to broken plural form by adding ائ between letters
Added ة to show this is a collective group noun
Added ال to specify these particular angels mentioned in the verse who take souls
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action of saying (يَقُولُونَ) in this verse
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a basic adjective
The shadda intensifies the meaning to emphasize complete purity

steps.m: those who are pure/good (referring to the righteous souls the angels are taking)
steps.n: Makes the word plural masculine to refer to multiple pure souls. This describes the state of the believers when the angels take them (تَتَوَفَّىٰهُمُ)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people (they) are doing the action
Root letters س-ل-م got short 'a' vowels (فتحة) to make the basic noun meaning 'peace'
Adding ا after ل makes it a stronger noun meaning 'peace/security'
Adding tanween (double damma) makes it 'a peace' instead of 'the peace'
The base word عَلَى changes its final ى to ي when connecting to pronouns. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to
Changed the ending vowel to ُ because this word connects to what comes after it (ٱدْخُلُوا۟)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed from 'he entered' to 'enter!' by adding u- at start and changing ending - makes it a command to one person

steps.m: enter! (command to many people) (connected to يَقُولُونَ - they say)
steps.n: Changed to plural command by adding وا and changed initial alif to waṣla form. This is what the angels say to the good people.
Added shadda to show emphasis on covering/concealing nature

steps.m: a garden (as an enclosed, protected space)
steps.n: Transforms into a noun for a specific enclosed space. The ة makes it feminine and specific.
Shows this is what is being entered (object of the command 'enter!')
Makes it refer to the specific Paradise rather than any garden. This is the object of the command 'Enter!'
This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it connects 'enter Paradise' with 'you used to do'.

steps.m: because of what/due to that which (connects to تَعْمَلُونَ)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show cause or reason. In this ayah, it explains they enter Paradise because of what they used to do.
Changed root letters into a command by adding damma (u) and sukun (stop mark) - means 'Be!'
Added past tense marker ت with damma to show 'you' did the action

steps.m: you all were (referring to those who did good deeds)
steps.n: Added م with sukun to make the verb plural, showing it refers to multiple people. This word connects to تَعْمَلُونَ (you used to do) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding يَ at the start and changing vowels shows this happens in the present/future instead of the past
Changing يَ to تَ at the start shows 'you' is doing the action instead of 'he'
Adding ون at the end shows many people are doing the action together
This is a question word that turns a statement into a yes/no question. In this ayah, it's asking if they are waiting/looking for something.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show present tense
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This verb comes after هَلْ which makes it a question 'do they look?'
This is the basic form of the word that shows something is being excluded from what was mentioned before
This is the same word - إِلَّا is just the standard spelling. The alif (ا) is always part of this word meaning 'except'
The آ is a longer version of ا that shows a pause in reading and connects this word to 'that they should be reached' (أَن تَأْتِيَهُمُ) which comes after it

This is a particle that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it connects what they are waiting for (يَنظُرُونَ) with what might come (تَأْتِيَهُمُ). It's like saying 'Are they waiting except that the angels should come to them?'
Connected the root letters together
Added ت at beginning and changed vowels to make it mean 'you/she come(s)' in present tense
Added هُمُ at the end to show the object (them)
Changed the root 'to rule/control' into a noun by adding vowels, making it mean 'angel'
This is a special plural form for angel, adding ائِ in the middle and ة at the end
Added ال to make it definite and ُ to show it's doing the action in the sentence. This word is the subject (doer) of تَأْتِيَهُمُ (comes to them).
This word connects two possibilities in this ayah: either the angels will come to them OR the command of their Lord will come. It shows these are alternative possibilities.
Added يَ at the beginning to show 'he' is doing the action in the present/future. Added ْ (sukun) after أ to help pronunciation.
Added َ (fatha) at the end to show this is in the subjunctive mood
Changed from basic root meaning 'command' into a noun meaning 'a command' by adding vowels
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb يَأْتِىَ (comes) in this ayah
Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to show complete mastery and lordship
Added ـِكَ (ika) at the end to mean 'your Lord'. The kasra connects رَبّ to the ك which makes it 'your'
The basic demonstrative word ذَا means 'this' and كَ at the beginning makes it stronger
Adding لِ to كَذَا makes it mean 'like' or 'similar to' instead of just 'this'

steps.m: like that (referring to what was mentioned before)
steps.n: The final كَ points away from the speaker, changing 'this' to 'that'

steps.m: he did (like those before them did)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to show this is something that happened in the past. This word refers to what the previous nations did, as mentioned in the ayah.
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ذ letter to show emphasis

steps.m: those who (referring to the disbelievers mentioned in previous verses)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to make it refer to multiple people
Basic preposition showing where something starts from or comes from. In this ayah, it connects to قَبْلِهِمْ (before them) to show 'from before them'
The kasra (ِ) under the م shows this word is connecting to what comes after it
Added fatha and sukun to create the noun meaning 'before'

steps.m: before them (refers to those who came before the disbelievers)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show this refers to 'them' (the previous people mentioned)
Added fatha to show this is the connecting word 'and'

steps.m: and not/and what (connects to ظَلَمَهُمُ - wronged them)
steps.n: Added ما which can mean 'not' or 'what' depending on context. Here it means 'not' because it's negating the verb ظَلَمَهُمُ (wronged them) in the phrase 'and Allah did not wrong them'

steps.m: to wrong, oppress, or treat unjustly

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added fathas to show this is something that happened in the past
Added هُمُ at the end to show who received the action (them). The ُ at the end is added because this connects to the next word اللَّهُ
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
Added damma to show this word is the subject
This is the basic word that shows contrast with what came before
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this contrasting idea with the previous statement
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action
Added optional pronunciation marks (the small و above the alif and the small circle) that don't change the meaning
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a basic noun
Changed to broken plural form by adding أ at start and changing vowels
Added هُمْ at the end to show possession/object. This word is being acted upon by the verb يَظْلِمُونَ (they wrong/oppress) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show it's happening now instead of in the past
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding the vowel 'aa' in the middle
Added أَ at the start to show something is affecting or happening to someone/something else (like hitting a target)
Added فَ to show this happened as a result of something before it. This word is connected to سَيِّـَٔاتُ (evil consequences) in the ayah.
Added هُمْ at the end to show who was struck
Root letters got vowels to make a noun meaning 'badness'
The weak letter و changes to ي and is doubled to show intensity
Added ات to make it plural, specifically referring to multiple bad actions or deeds
Added ُ to show this word is doing the action of striking/afflicting in the sentence

steps.m: that which (refers to سَيِّـَٔاتُ - evil consequences)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of the sentence. Here it refers back to سَيِّـَٔاتُ (evil consequences) and connects it to عَمِلُوا (they did/earned).
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make past tense singular form

steps.m: they did (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added plural masculine ending وا to show multiple people did the action. The small و above (۟) is just a pronunciation mark.
The middle letter ي changes to ا, and we add fatha marks to show it happened in the past

steps.m: and surrounded/encompassed (connected to فَأَصَابَهُمْ - then it struck them)
steps.n: Adds و at the beginning to connect this action with the previous one in the verse
This is a preposition that shows how something is done or connected

steps.m: with them (refers to those who mocked)
steps.n: Added هِم which means 'them' to show who is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to those who used to mock.
This is a standalone relative pronoun/particle, not derived from a root. No morphological transformation needed.
Added shadda to emphasize the م sound. In this ayah, it connects the verb عَمِلُوا (they did) to سَيِّـَٔاتُ (evil consequences), showing what they did.
Adding the fatha (َ) makes it a past tense verb
Adding another fatha (َ) completes the past tense verb pattern
Added وا at the end to change 'was' to 'they were' (showing multiple people)

steps.m: with it (referring to what they used to mock)
steps.n: Connected the preposition ب (with) to the pronoun ه (it) and added vowels. This shows what they were mocking with.

steps.m: with it (referring to what they used to mock)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ۦ) after ه to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added است to show intensive or exaggerated mocking
Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action
Added short vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he said'
Changed قَوَلَ to قَالَ by replacing the weak letter و with ا
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
This is a relative pronoun used to describe a group of people
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Adding أ (hamza) at the start changes the meaning to 'make something/someone a partner' instead of just 'partnering'. Like turning 'partner' into 'make partner'.
