steps.m: indeed, certainly (introduces what Allah sent down)
steps.n: Adding the shadda makes the emphasis stronger. This word introduces what Allah sent down in this ayah, making the statement that follows more certain and emphasized.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. Here it connects 'sign' (ءَايَةً) with what comes after it.
The ي changes to ى at the end of words in Arabic writing. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
This is the basic pointing word used to indicate something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show this points to something farther away
Adding ل with kasra (ِ) changes 'this' to 'that', making it point to something more distant or previously mentioned

steps.m: that (referring to the rain giving life to dead earth)
steps.n: The كَ at the end makes this word specifically point to something that was just mentioned in the ayah - in this case, how Allah sends down rain to give life to dead earth

steps.m: to show, to be clear, to be apparent

steps.n: Adding ة at the end makes this a feminine noun meaning 'sign'
Adding ل makes it 'for/to a sign' - shows purpose or ownership

steps.m: surely a sign (object of إِنَّ فِى)
steps.n: The word gets a tanween (ً) at the end to show it's connected to إِنَّ فِى (surely in) - like saying 'surely in...a sign'
Changes verb root into a noun meaning 'a group that stands together'
Added لِ at the beginning to show this group is the recipient or purpose of something (in this case, they are who the sign is for)

steps.m: for a people (who listen to the signs)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل and tanween at the end. This word connects to يَسْمَعُونَ (they listen) which comes after it, showing these are people who listen to Allah's signs.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Adding يَ at start and changing vowels shows this is happening now rather than in the past
Adding ونَ changes the subject from 'he' to 'they' (masculine plural)
This is a special word used to emphasize what comes after it, like saying 'indeed' or 'certainly'

steps.m: and indeed (connects to previous verse while emphasizing what follows)
steps.n: Added conjunction prefix وَ (and) to connect with previous statement
This is a pronoun that means 'you' when talking to multiple people. The ُ (damma) and مْ (sukun on meem) together show it's plural.

steps.m: for you all (connected to عِبْرَةً - a lesson)
steps.n: Added لَ at the beginning to show this is for or belongs to the group being addressed. In this ayah, it connects to the word عِبْرَةً (a lesson) to show the lesson is for them.
This is a preposition that shows something is located inside something else. In this ayah, it specifically shows that something is inside the cattle.
The ي changes to ى because it's written in the Uthmani script style of the Quran. This doesn't change the meaning or pronunciation.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the base word meaning livestock, which were seen as a blessing
Changed نَعَم to plural by adding أ at start and putting ا after ع to make أنعام, which means 'cattle'
Added ال at beginning to make it definite (like 'the' in English)
Added kasra at the end to show this word follows 'in' (فِى) in the ayah

steps.m: to draw lessons from something by thinking about it

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a noun by adding the vowel 'i' and sukoon marks to make the word mean 'lesson'
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of a lesson
Added لَ at beginning to emphasize that this is definitely/surely a lesson
Added the -an sound (tanween) to show this is what we're learning from or taking a lesson from
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Changed to present tense 'we' form with prefix نُ and vowel pattern changes
Added emphasis with shadda on the نُ

steps.m: we give drink to you all (with emphasis)
steps.n: Added suffix كُم to show who receives the action (you all)
Combined the two basic words مِن (from) and مَا (what) together
The ن in مِن changes to match the م that follows it, creating a doubled م sound (shown by the ّ mark). This is a common pronunciation rule in Arabic when certain letters come together.
Basic preposition showing that something is inside or within something else. Here it connects 'the cattle' (ٱلْأَنْعٰمِ) with 'a lesson' (لَعِبْرَةً), showing where the lesson can be found.

steps.m: to be inside or contain something

steps.n: Changes the root word into a plural noun meaning 'bellies' by adding damma marks (ُ) and ون at the end
Adds ِ to show these bellies belong to something (in this case, the cattle)
Adds هِۦ to show these are the bellies of the cattle mentioned earlier (الأنعام)

steps.m: from (connecting بُطُونِهِۦ with what comes after)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it shows that something comes from inside the animals' bellies (بُطُونِهِۦ).
When مِنۢ combines with the word بَيْنِ that follows it, they together mean 'from between' - showing something coming from the space between two things (in this case, between waste matter and blood)
Changes from verb 'to separate' into noun meaning 'between'
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to 'waste' that comes after it in the ayah

steps.m: to digest or break down food in stomach

steps.n: Changed the action into a noun for the actual substance that results from digestion
Added tanween kasra to show this word is possessed by بَيْنِ (between) in the phrase 'from between partly digested food and blood'
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic noun
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with فَرْثٍ (waste matter)
Added tanween (two dots under) to show this is 'some blood' - connects to the word before it (فَرْثٍ)
Added fatha vowels to make the basic noun form meaning milk
The ل gets doubled (shadda) because it comes right after the word 'we give you to drink' - this is a common Arabic rule when certain letters meet

steps.m: milk (as the object of نُّسْقِيكُم - we give you to drink)
steps.n: Added -an ending to show milk is the thing being given to drink
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed from verb 'became pure' to noun meaning 'one who is pure'
Adds tanween to show this word is describing the milk (لَّبَنًا) mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: to go down easily (like food or drink)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes from verb (went down easily) to describing something that goes down easily
Spelling variation - long alif written as آ instead of ا
Added tanween (ًا) to match the case of لَبَنًا that it describes
Added alif after first root letter and kasra after second root letter to show this is someone who drinks
Added ين at the end to make it plural (meaning many drinkers)
Added لِ at start to mean 'for' or 'to'
Added another ل to make 'the' (combining with previous ل to mean 'for the')
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from or is taken from something else. Here it connects to ثَمَرٰتِ (fruits) in the ayah.
The و adds the meaning of 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic noun for 'fruit'
Added the feminine plural ending to show multiple fruits. This word is connected to 'palm trees' (النَّخِيلِ) in the ayah.

steps.m: fruits of (belonging to النَّخِيلِ - palm trees)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to or is connected to something else (in this case, palm trees)

steps.m: to extract or select (like separating good from bad)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed to noun pattern showing 'many palm trees' by adding ي
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ن with shadda

steps.m: of the palm trees (connected to ثَمَرٰتِ - fruits)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to 'fruits' (ثَمَرٰتِ) before it in the ayah
Adds vowels to make the basic noun form for 'grapes'
Changed from singular 'grape' to plural 'grapes' using the pattern أفعال (af'aal)
Added ال to make it specific grapes
Added kasra at the end to show it's connected to the word before it (ثَمَرٰتِ)
Added و at beginning to connect it with the previous word (ٱلنَّخِيلِ)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding تَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from simple 'taking' to 'adopting/making use of' by adding ت with shadda - makes the action more deliberate and personal
Added ون to show that multiple people are doing the action
This is a preposition that shows something comes from or originates from something else

steps.m: from it (referring to the fruits of date-palms and grapes)
steps.n: Added the pronoun ه with damma (هُ) at the end to show what we're getting something from. Here it refers back to the fruits mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added vowel marks to create a noun meaning the substance that causes intoxication
Added tanween alif to show this word is the object being taken/used in the verse
Changes the verb (to provide) into a noun (provision/sustenance) by adding specific vowel patterns
Added وَ (wa) at the beginning to connect this with سَكَرًا (intoxicant) from the previous part

steps.m: and a provision (object of تَتَّخِذُونَ - you take/make)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this is something being acted upon by the verb تَتَّخِذُونَ (you take/make). It's like saying 'you make it into a provision'

steps.m: to be good, beautiful, or excellent

steps.n: Root letters get vowels to make a simple word meaning 'good'

steps.m: good/beautiful (as a description of رِزْقًا - provision)
steps.n: Added tanwin alif (ًا) to show this word is describing how something is. Here it's describing رِزْقًا (provision) as being good.

Adding the shadda (double letter mark) on ن changes this from a basic word meaning 'if/that' into a stronger word that adds emphasis to what comes next in the sentence. It's like adding 'truly' or 'certainly' in English.
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. Here it connects 'a sign' (ءَايَةً) with 'that' (ذٰلِكَ) to mean 'in that is a sign'
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif is replaced with a long alif (ٰ) to show distance
Added ل with kasra to strengthen the pointing meaning

steps.m: that (pointing to something in the previous statement)
steps.n: Added ك with fatha to show this is pointing back to what was just mentioned (the fruits and drinks from palm trees and grapevines)
The root letters combine with vowels to form the base word. The hamza gets separated with an alif due to pronunciation rules.
The ة shows this is one specific instance of a sign
The -an ending (tanween) shows this is 'a sign' (not 'the sign') and marks it as the main thing إِنَّ is talking about
The لَ adds emphasis because it comes after إِنَّ, showing this is definitely true

steps.m: to stand firmly or rise up

steps.n: Changes verb 'to stand' into noun meaning 'those who stand together' -> 'people'
Added لِ (meaning 'for/to') with extra emphasis (shown by ّ )
Added tanween to show this refers to any group of people who understand, connecting it to the verb يَعْقِلُونَ (they understand) that follows
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now, changed vowels to show ongoing action

steps.m: they understand/reason (referring to people who reflect on Allah's signs)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to people who reflect on Allah's signs in nature.

steps.m: he inspired/revealed (subject of رَبُّكَ - your Lord)
steps.n: Added hamza with fatha (أَ) at start and و with sukun (وْ) after first letter to show someone making something happen. The ى at the end shows it happened in the past.

steps.m: and he inspired/revealed (subject of رَبُّكَ - your Lord)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it in the verse

steps.m: and he inspired/revealed (subject of رَبُّكَ - your Lord)
steps.n: Added small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's pronounced like a long a sound
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this an intensive active participle, showing someone who does the nurturing action completely
Adding damma shows this word is doing the action in the sentence - it is the subject of the verb أَوْحَىٰ (inspired/revealed)
Adding 2nd person masculine singular possessive pronoun suffix. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb أَوْحَىٰ (inspired/revealed).
This is a basic preposition showing direction. In this ayah, it connects 'inspired' (وَأَوْحَىٰ) with 'the bee' (ٱلنَّحْلِ) to show that the inspiration was directed to the bee.

steps.m: to give generously, to present gifts

steps.n: The root meaning evolved to refer to bees, possibly because they give the gift of honey. This creates the basic noun form.
Added the definite article ال and doubled the ن with shadda as required when adding ال to words starting with ن
Changed initial alif to wasl form and added kasra at end because it follows the preposition إلى (to) in إِلَى ٱلنَّحْلِ (to the bee)
This is a connecting word that joins what comes before with what follows. Here it connects 'He inspired' (وَأَوْحَىٰ) with the command that follows.
The ِ (kasra) is added because this word introduces a command. This helps connect it smoothly to the command that follows.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
The ت with shadda shows the action is being done reflexively (to oneself). The initial alif changes to match the new pattern.
Changed from past tense 'he took' to a command 'take!' by changing the middle vowel from 'a' to 'i'
Added ي at the end to make it a command for a female (like saying 'you take!' to a girl), and changed the beginning alif
Basic particle that shows where something comes from or what it's made of
Added َ (fatha) at the end to connect smoothly to the next word الْجِبَالِ (the mountains). This shows the bees should take from the mountains.
Changes from single mountain to many mountains

steps.m: the mountains (that the bees use to build homes in)
steps.n: Added 'al' to make it specific and kasra at the end because it follows مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah
Changed the internal vowels and added و to make it plural. This is a common way to make plurals for words about buildings and spaces.
Added ًا to show these houses are being made or taken. This connects to the word اتَّخِذِى (take/make) earlier in the verse.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it's showing that the bees should take from the trees.

steps.m: and from (connects to ٱلشَّجَرِ - the trees)
steps.n: The و at the beginning connects this phrase to the previous mention of mountains (ٱلْجِبَالِ), showing bees should take from both mountains and trees

steps.m: and from (connects to ٱلشَّجَرِ - the trees)
steps.n: Added a fatha at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word ٱلشَّجَرِ (the trees)
The root forms a noun meaning the thing that grows thick with branches
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and form the basic noun
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ش with shadda because it comes after ال
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from) in the ayah
Starting with the basic preposition 'from'
The word مِن (from) combines with ما (what) to become مِمَّا (from what). The م gets doubled with a shadda to make it flow better when speaking.
Added و at the beginning to connect this to the previous phrase about the bees making homes. This word refers to what the bees build.

steps.m: to build or construct (especially raised structures like trellises)
steps.n: The root meaning becomes concrete as a noun
The يَ at the start shows this is happening in the present time, and the ِ (kasra) on ر shows it's an active verb

steps.m: they build (referring to the bees building their hives)
steps.n: The ون at the end shows that a group (in this case, the bees) are doing this action together

steps.m: then (connects this command to eat from fruits with what came before)
steps.n: This word connects actions in sequence, showing that one thing happens after another. In this ayah, it connects the command to eat from fruits with previous instructions.
Changed from 'to eat' to 'eat!' by making it a command - just like saying 'eat your food!'
Added ى at the end to make it a feminine command, since Allah is speaking to the female bee
Added kasra (ِ) under م to show this word connects 'eat' to 'all fruits', telling the bees which things to eat from
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word connects to 'fruits' (الثَّمَرٰتِ) that comes after it
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Adds ة at the end to change from general 'fruit' to mean 'one single fruit'
This is the regular feminine plural showing multiple fruits. The word comes after the preposition مِن (from) which puts it in the genitive case.
Added 'the' at the beginning with ٱل, doubled the ث because it's a sun letter, and added ِ at the end because it comes after the word مِن (from)

steps.m: to follow a path or way

steps.n: Changed to command form by adding sukoon marks - tells one person to follow a path
Added helper alif with damma to make the command easier to pronounce
Added ي with kasra to make it a command for a female
Added فَ (so/then) to connect with previous command 'eat' (كُلِى). The alif changed to hamza-wasl (ٱ) after فَ.

steps.m: to travel along a path or way

steps.n: Changes the root into a plural noun meaning multiple paths by adding damma vowels
Shows this word is being acted upon by the command 'follow' (فَٱسْلُكِى) in the ayah
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and takes care of others perfectly

steps.m: one who nurtures completely (connected to كِ)
steps.n: The kasra (ِ) shows this word connects to something after it (in this case كِ meaning 'your')
The كِ shows this is talking to a female (in this case, the bee mentioned earlier in the verse)
Changed the root into a plural noun by adding damma vowels, meaning 'paths that are made easy to travel'

steps.m: made easy (plural, as object of فَٱسْلُكِى)
steps.n: Added tanween (two damma marks) and alif to show this word is describing the paths that the bees follow
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on first root letter, and damma on second root letter. This word describes what شَرَابٌ (drink) does.
Basic preposition showing where something comes from. In this ayah, it shows that something comes out from their bellies.
Added a small ن (called nun ghunna) at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to be inside or contain within

steps.n: Changes the root word into plural 'bellies' by adding ُ marks (damma) on the ب and ط, and adding و at the end
Added ِ at the end because it comes after the word مِنۢ (from) in the ayah
Added هَا at the end to show these are the bellies of the bees mentioned earlier in the ayah
Changes from verb 'to drink' to noun 'drink' by adding long vowel after second root letter

steps.m: a drink (that emerges from يَخْرُجُ مِنۢ)
steps.n: Added double-damma (tanween) to show this is any drink, not a specific one. This drink is what comes out, showing its role in the sentence.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
The word changed to show something becoming different on its own (like milk turning sour), rather than being made different by someone else
Changed from past tense verb (happened) to active participle (describing word) + added tanween
Changed verb concept into a noun for a single color
Changed single color into plural form for many colors
Added ُ to show this word is doing the action of being different (subject of مُّخْتَلِفٌ - varying/different)
Added هُۥ to show these are the colors belonging to it (the honey mentioned earlier)
Added kasra under ف to show it's a preposition meaning 'in'

steps.m: in it (referring to شَرَابٌ - the drink)
steps.n: Added ه with kasra at the end to show this means 'in it', referring back to the drink (شَرَابٌ) mentioned earlier in the verse
Changed from basic root meaning 'heal' into the noun 'healing' by adding vowels (kasra, fatha) and an alif at the end
Added hamza at the end to make it a complete noun meaning 'healing'
The tanween (double damma) makes it 'a healing' instead of 'the healing'

steps.m: to be friendly or familiar with others
people (those who are social/familiar with each other)
steps.n: Changed from a verb about being social into a noun meaning 'people' by adding a long 'aa' sound in the middle
Added ال (the) at the start to make it 'the people' instead of just 'people'
Added لِ meaning 'for/to' and kasra at the end to show connection. This word shows who receives the healing (شِفَآءٌ) mentioned in the ayah.
Added shadda on ل for emphasis and easier pronunciation
Basic emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
Added shadda and kasra to strengthen the emphasis. This particle emphasizes that there is definitely healing (شِفَآءٌ) in the honey.
Basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it connects 'healing' (شِفَآءٌ) with 'it' (هِ), showing the healing is inside the honey.
Basic demonstrative pronoun for something close
The alif is written with a special mark (dagger alif) above the ذ
Adding لِ changes it from pointing to something close to pointing to something far away

steps.m: that (pointing to what was mentioned before)
steps.n: Adding كَ makes it specifically refer back to something mentioned before - in this case, referring back to what was just described about the bees and honey
The first two root letters combine into a long آ sound, which is common with this type of word
The ة (taa marboota) marks this as a feminine singular noun
The لَ adds emphasis meaning 'surely/certainly'. The ً (tanwin) at the end shows this is indefinite (like 'a' in English). This word refers back to what was mentioned before (ذٰلِكَ/that)

steps.m: to stand together, rise up as one

steps.n: The root meaning of standing/rising became associated with a group of people who stand together
Adds -in sound at the end to show it belongs to/is owned by something
Adds 'for/to' meaning with emphasis
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Adding ت at start and doubling middle letter shows someone doing this thinking intensively to themselves
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
This is the proper name of God in Arabic. It comes from the root meaning of divinity and worship, but has become a unique proper noun.
Added shadda on ل to show it is doubled in pronunciation

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of خَلَقَكُمْ - created you)
steps.n: The ُ (damma) at the end shows that Allah is doing the action in this sentence - specifically creating (خَلَقَ)

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of خَلَقَكُمْ - created you)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before it

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of خَلَقَكُمْ - created you)
steps.n: Changed the alif to a wasla form (ٱ) since it connects in pronunciation with the و before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show Allah did this action in the past. This is what Allah (وَٱللَّهُ) did.

steps.m: He created you all (object of Allah's creation)
steps.n: Added كُمْ (kum) at the end to mean 'you all' - this shows Allah created all of you, not just one person
Basic form of the connecting word that shows the next event in sequence. Here it connects Allah's creation of humans to Him causing their death.
Added damma (ُ) and shadda (ّ) to show proper pronunciation
Adding vowel marks (fatha) turns the root letters into the past tense 'he' form
Doubling of middle root letter intensifies meaning

steps.m: to be taken completely (as in death)
steps.n: Adding ت prefix shows this happens to someone/something rather than someone doing it

steps.m: he takes completely (referring to Allah taking souls)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at the start. In this ayah, Allah is the subject doing the taking.

steps.m: He (Allah) takes your souls (causes you to die)
steps.n: Adds كُمْ to show who is being taken - refers to all humans Allah causes to die
Starting with the basic preposition that means 'from' or 'among'
Added كُم at the end to show this applies to a group of people being spoken to
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
This is the basic form of the word that refers to people in questions or statements
The shadda is added because this word combines with the next letter. It's the subject (doer) of the verb 'is returned' that comes after it.

steps.m: to return something or send back

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Combined the repeated د into one letter with shadda to show emphasis

steps.m: he is being returned (to أَرْذَلِ ٱلْعُمُرِ - the worst part of life)
steps.n: Added يُ at the beginning to show this is happening to someone rather than someone doing it. In this ayah, it's talking about some people being returned to the worst part of life (old age).
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show this is in the nominative case
Basic preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it points towards 'the worst state of life' (أَرْذَلِ).
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end which is a writing variation of alif (ا) when it comes at the end of words
Added a small alif (ٓ) above the word to show that the sound is stretched slightly when reading
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Added أَ at beginning and sukun on first root letter to create superlative form. This describes the worst state of something, in this case referring to the weakest state of age/life.
Added kasra at the end because this word is being acted upon by إِلَىٰٓ (to) in the phrase 'to the worst state of age'
Adding damma vowels changes the verb 'to live long' into the noun 'age/lifespan'
Added 'the' at the start and kasra at the end because it follows the preposition إِلَىٰٓ (to/towards)
Basic particle that shows purpose or intention

steps.m: so that/in order to (connects to لَا يَعْلَمَ)
steps.n: Added لِ at beginning to strengthen the meaning of purpose. This combination introduces the purpose of what was mentioned before - Allah returning some to a feeble age.

steps.m: so that/in order to (connects to لَا يَعْلَمَ)
steps.n: Changed the final ي to ى with sukun (ْ) for pronunciation. This is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَعْلَمَ (knows) into its opposite meaning (does not know). It's like adding 'not' before a verb in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed ending vowel to fatha to show subjunctive mood
Root meaning 'distance' becomes a word for 'after' by adding vowels - like saying something comes at a distance in time
Added fatha at the end to show this word connects to 'knowledge' (عِلْمٍ) that comes after it in the ayah
Changes the action of knowing into the thing itself (knowledge). Like how 'to know' becomes 'knowledge' in English.

Adds two small diagonal lines (called kasratain) at the end to show this word comes after بَعْدَ (meaning 'after') in the sentence. The kasratain also shows we're talking about 'any knowledge' rather than 'the knowledge'.
Connected the letters with vowel marks to make a basic noun. This word is what is being known/learned in the verb يَعْلَمَ (to know).
Added tanween alif (ـًا) to show this is the object being acted upon. The hamza changes position due to writing rules.
Adding shadda (ّ) changes this from a conditional 'if' to an emphatic 'indeed'. This emphasizes the following statement that 'Allah is All-Knowing, Most Capable'.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows the ل is doubled.
Added fatha at the end because Allah is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily) in this ayah. Also changed initial alif to wasl form since it connects to previous word.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to know'
Added ي between second and third root letters to show this is a quality or characteristic

steps.m: All-Knowing (as a divine attribute of اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added tanween (double damma) to show this is describing Allah's perfect attribute of knowledge
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters and changed middle vowel to kasra to show intense quality
Added tanwin (double damma) to show this is describing Allah in this ayah
This is a unique divine name that started from the root meaning of divinity/worship but became the proper name for the One God. The double ل with shadda is special to this word.
Added the shadda mark on ل to show it is doubled, and fatha on it to show how it's pronounced
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject (doer) of the action فَضَّلَ (favored) in this ayah

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of فَضَّلَ/favored)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement, and added the helper alif (ٱ) that comes with ال
Basic past tense verb form with fatha vowels
Added shadda (doubling mark) on middle letter to show intensity. This word describes what Allah (وَٱللَّهُ) did - He gave greater preference to some over others in provision.

steps.m: to divide into parts or portions

steps.n: Added fatha and sukun to make the noun meaning 'some' or 'a portion'. This word receives the action of Allah's favoring (فَضَّلَ) in the phrase 'Allah has favored some...'

steps.m: some of you (object of فَضَّلَ - favored)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this refers to 'you all'. The whole phrase means 'Allah has favored some of you over others in provision'
This is a basic preposition that shows something is physically or metaphorically above something else
Added the small alif (ىٰ) at the end to show this is a stopping point in reading. In this ayah, it shows some people having authority over others in terms of provision (رِزْق).
Added vowel marks to make the basic noun form meaning 'some' or 'part'

steps.m: some/part (being compared with others in رِزْقِ)
steps.n: Added the -in sound at the end to show it's connected to another word (رِزْقِ) meaning 'provision'
Basic preposition showing location or containment
Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. In this ayah, it shows that something is 'in provision/sustenance (الرِّزْقِ)'
Changes from verb 'to provide' into noun meaning 'provision'
Adding ٱل (the) makes it refer to a specific provision rather than provision in general
The ِ at the end shows this word follows فِى (in/concerning) in the ayah
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what' that connects to the verb فُضِّلُوا۟ (they were favored) in this ayah
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement. It connects the question 'what' to the earlier mention of Allah favoring some over others.
Added kasra and ي to create the basic relative pronoun
Added ين at the end to make it plural - now means 'those who' instead of 'one who'

steps.m: those who (subject of فُضِّلُوا۟ - were favored)
steps.n: Added ٱل with shadda to make it definite and point to specific people. This word is describing those who were given more provision, as it's the subject of فُضِّلُوا۟ (were favored)
Added shadda (double letter) to show doing something extra special for someone
Changed vowels to show passive voice - the action being done to someone

steps.m: they were favored (refers to those who were given more provision)
steps.n: Added plural marker to show multiple people were favored. This refers back to those mentioned earlier in the ayah who were given more provision.
Forms active participle showing who does the action
The alif is lengthened to help with pronunciation
Adds preposition meaning 'with/by' as prefix
Adds ى to show this describes something related to giving back

steps.m: to provide with what is needed to live

steps.n: Changed from verb (providing) to noun (the actual food/provisions) by adding vowel marks
Added ِ at the end to show this word belongs to or connects with what comes next

steps.m: their provision (referring to those who were favored with more)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show this belongs to them. This refers back to those mentioned earlier in the verse who were given more provision than others.
This is a basic preposition showing one thing being above or having authority over another. In this ayah, it shows how some people are given more provision than others.
Added an alif symbol (ٰ) after the ى which helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning
This is a relative pronoun used to refer to things or concepts. In this ayah, it specifically refers back to the provisions/wealth (ٱلرِّزْقِ) mentioned earlier.

steps.m: to own or have complete control over something

steps.n: Added the vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: she owned/possessed (refers to what أَيْمٰنُهُمْ owns)
steps.n: Added ت with sukun at the end to show a female subject did this action. This word is connected to أَيْمٰنُهُمْ (their right hands) which is feminine in Arabic.

steps.m: to be on the right side or direction

steps.n: Added vowels to the root letters to make the word mean 'right hand/side'
Changes the word to show many right hands/sides using a special plural pattern
Changed alif to long alif (ٰ) and added damma for grammar
Added هُمْ to show these belong to them
Added diacritics to show proper pronunciation of the pronoun 'they'

Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before. In this ayah, it's connecting to the previous statement about provision/wealth, indicating 'so as a result, they are equal in this matter.'
Basic preposition meaning 'in' or 'within'
Added the pronoun هِ (it) to show what is being referred to. Here it refers back to رِزْق (provision) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added fatha vowels between the letters and changed the last letter to alif to show something is equal
Added hamza after the alif to make it into a noun describing a quality
Added two dots on top (tanween) to show this word is the main thing being talked about in the phrase 'in it'
Changes the concept of giving blessings into a specific blessing itself
The kasra shows this blessing belongs to Allah, connecting it to اللَّهِ later in the ayah

steps.m: so/then of a blessing (possessed by اللَّهِ)
steps.n: The فَ connects this to the previous statement about Allah giving more provision to some than others

steps.m: Is it then of a blessing (possessed by اللَّهِ)?
steps.n: The أَ makes this a rhetorical question, expressing disapproval of denying Allah's blessings mentioned before

steps.m: Is it then by/with a blessing (possessed by اللَّهِ)?
steps.n: Preposition بِ shows they are questioning about using/benefiting from Allah's blessing. This connects to يَجْحَدُونَ (they deny) which follows in the ayah.
Added shadda to show emphasis on the ل
Changed first letter to ٱ and added kasra at end to show possession. This word is possessed by نِعْمَة (favor) in the phrase 'favor of Allah'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes from past tense 'denied' to present tense 'denies' - shows ongoing action of denying Allah's favors
Shows that multiple people are denying Allah's favors, referring back to those who were given more provisions in the ayah
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity
Added 'L' sound (shadda) to show this is not just any god, but the one true God - Allah
Added a small diagonal line (fatha) above the letter ل to show it's pronounced with an 'a' sound
Changed alif to wasl alif for proper connection with previous words
Added damma to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb جَعَلَ (made/created)
Added و at beginning to connect this with previous statement

steps.m: to make or set something in a certain way
he made (connected to لَكُم meaning 'for you')
steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word connects to لَكُم which tells us who it was made for.
Added fatha to show the basic meaning of direction or purpose

steps.m: for you all (object of جَعَلَ - He made)
steps.n: Added كُم to show this is being done for multiple people. This word shows who benefits from Allah's action of making (جَعَلَ) in this verse.
Added sukun at the end to show where the word stops
Added shadda because this word comes after a word ending in ن or tanwin, making the م sound stronger. This is just a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning.
Added أ at beginning and changed middle vowel to make it plural
Added كُمْ at the end to show possession by multiple people
Root meaning 'pair/join' becomes noun meaning 'spouse' with vowel pattern
Made plural by adding أَ at the start and ا in the middle - like changing 'spouse' to 'spouses'

steps.m: spouses (as the object of جَعَلَ - made)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this word is being acted upon by the verb جَعَلَ (made/created)

steps.m: to make something into something else, to set, to place

steps.n: Adding fathas connects the letters and shows this happened in the past. This is the main verb telling what Allah did.

steps.m: and he made/placed (connected to جَعَلَ earlier in 16:72)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect this with Allah's earlier action of making spouses for people
Added fatha to the letter lam to show it's connected to what comes after it. This word shows who Allah made something for in this ayah.

steps.m: for you all (object of جَعَلَ - made)
steps.n: Added كُم at the end to show this is being done for 'you all'. The damma (ُ) on ك shows this is for multiple people being spoken to.
This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts
The shadda (ّ) appears on م because it comes after a word ending in ن, causing the two similar sounds to merge. This is just a pronunciation change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Root meaning 'pair/join' becomes noun meaning 'spouse' with vowel pattern
The word becomes plural by adding أ at the start and changing the middle to وا, meaning 'many pairs/spouses' instead of just one
The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) for style, but meaning stays the same
Added كُم at the end to show the spouses belong to multiple people being spoken to
Added vowel marks to form the singular noun 'son' - someone who continues the family foundation
Added ن with fatha at the end to make the word plural, showing multiple sons

steps.m: to serve or assist quickly and actively

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Added ة to create a noun referring to those who serve/assist (which came to mean grandchildren as they often help their grandparents)
Added و at beginning to connect this word to the previous word (بَنِينَ) in the list

steps.m: and grandchildren (as a direct object of جَعَلَ/made)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this word is receiving the action of Allah 'making' in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make past tense verb. This continues the actions that Allah did, after جَعَلَ (he made) in the ayah.
Added و at beginning to connect this action with the previous ones Allah did

steps.m: and he provided you all (subject of جَعَلَ)
steps.n: Added كُم at the end to show who received the provision - you all
Added sukun mark to show end of word
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows something comes from somewhere else
The noon of مِن assimilates with the following lam in الطَّيِّبٰتِ, resulting in a shadda on the meem. This is just a pronunciation change.
The fatha is added at the end to connect this word grammatically with الطَّيِّبٰتِ (the good things) that follows it
Added vowel marks and shadda to form basic adjective
Makes feminine plural form to indicate multiple good things
Adds definite article ٱل with assimilation causing shadda on ط

steps.m: of the good things (object of مِنَ)
steps.n: Changes the ending to match مِنَ (from) which comes before it, showing it's connected to that word
Changes from verb 'to be false' to noun meaning 'that which is false'

steps.m: the falsehood (in genitive case after بِ)
steps.n: Added 'the' (ال) to make it specific + changed ending due to coming after بِ
Added preposition بِ to show how they believe - with/by falsehood
Added فَ to show this follows from previous statement

steps.m: Is it then with/by the falsehood (that they believe)?
steps.n: Added أَ at start to make it a question expressing disapproval

steps.m: to trust, believe in, feel secure

steps.n: Changed root to Form IV verb (added alif) to mean 'actively believe/have faith' instead of just 'feel secure'
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
Changes from 'giving blessings' (verb) to 'one blessing' (noun)
Adds بِ to show how something is done, and changes the ending to show it's connected to the next word (اللَّهِ)
Adds conjunction 'and'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters with shadda
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word نِعْمَت (blessings) before it, showing these are Allah's blessings

This is a pronoun that shows we're talking about multiple people (all male or mixed group). In this ayah, it's the object of the verb يَكْفُرُونَ (they disbelieve), showing who is disbelieving in Allah's blessings.
Added vowel marks to make 'he' past tense verb meaning 'disbelieved'
Converting past tense verb to present tense by adding the present tense prefix ي
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding يَ at the start and changing middle vowel to ُ shows the action is happening now or in the future
Adding ون shows multiple people are doing the action. This word is connected to يَمْلِكُ (possesses/owns) in this ayah.
Adding و connects this to the previous statement

This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it connects the word دُونِ (besides/other than) with ٱللَّهِ (Allah), forming the phrase 'besides/other than Allah'
Adding damma (ُ) to make it a complete word
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes after it, forming the phrase 'besides Allah'
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One True God. The hamza is dropped and the lam gets a shadda.
Added kasra at the end to show possession. This word is the object of the phrase مِن دُونِ (besides/other than) in this ayah.
In this ayah, مَا means 'that which' and refers to what they worship besides Allah. It is the object (what is being acted upon) of the verb يَعْبُدُونَ (they worship).
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَمْلِكُ (possesses/owns) into 'does not possess/own'. It's like adding 'not' in English to make something negative.
Added a fatha mark (َ) on the ل to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'laa'
Basic vowels (a-a-a) added to make it 'he' past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing vowels, and adding ُ at end. This word comes after لا (not) in the ayah, so it means 'does not possess'.
Added fatha to show the correct pronunciation
Added هُ which means 'him/it'

steps.m: for/to them (referring to the false gods they worship)
steps.n: Changed هُ to هُمْ to show it refers to multiple things/people. In this ayah, it refers to the false gods that people worship instead of Allah.

steps.m: to provide or sustain with resources

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun by adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter

steps.m: any provision/sustenance (as object being acted upon)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is the object receiving the action in the sentence
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows something comes from somewhere else
The م gets doubled with a shadda to add emphasis, like saying 'directly from' or 'truly from'
The fatha at the end shows this connects to the next word السَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens), like saying 'from the heavens'
Added vowels to connect the root letters
The root word meaning 'rise up' changed to mean 'sky' by adding a long 'aa' sound and changing the last letter to hamza
Changes sky/heaven into plural by adding وات at the end
Adds definite article to make the noun definite
Added kasra at the end to show this word follows مِنَ (from) in the ayah. Also changed regular alif to alif maqsurah for Quranic spelling.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun
Added 'al' (ال) at start to mean 'the earth' and kasra at end to show it's connected to the word 'from' (مِنَ) before it

steps.m: and of/from the earth (connected to رِزْقًا مِّنَ)
steps.n: Adding conjunction و to connect with previous phrase
The root letters ش-ي-أ become the noun 'thing' by adding the vowel marks fatha (َ) and sukun (ْ). In this ayah, it's used as the object - meaning it's the thing that is not owned.
Added tanween fatha (ًا) to show this is the object being acted upon. The word is indefinite (not specific) because it's in a negative context - meaning 'not any thing'.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this negative phrase to the previous statement about what they possess
Adds يَ (he) at start and ُ at end to make present tense 'he' form
Added 'ist' (سْتَ) in the middle to show trying or seeking ability

steps.m: they are able (to provide sustenance from heaven and earth)
steps.n: Adding masculine plural suffix to indicate multiple male subjects. In this ayah, it refers to false deities who are not able to provide sustenance from heaven and earth.
This is the basic negative particle that stops something from happening. Here it tells people not to do the action in تَضْرِبُوا (make comparisons).
Added فَ at the beginning to show this comes as a result of something before it. It's like saying 'so because of that, don't do this' or 'therefore don't do this'.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from past tense 'he struck' to present tense 'you strike' by adding ت at start and changing middle vowel

steps.m: you all strike/make comparisons (addressing multiple people)
steps.n: The word changes to mean 'you all' (plural) instead of just 'you' (singular), and works with لِلَّهِ ٱلْأَمْثَالَ to mean 'do not make comparisons for Allah'
This is the basic word for deity or object of worship
Adding shadda on ل makes this the proper name for The One True God
Adding لِ at the beginning shows this belongs to or is for Allah. The ا from ال is dropped when combined with لِ

steps.m: for/to Allah (being compared to in الْأَمْثَالَ)
steps.n: The ِ (kasra) at the end is just part of how this word is normally written - it's not related to being compared to الْأَمْثَالَ. The word simply means 'for/to Allah'
Root letters got vowels (fat-ha) to make a noun meaning 'example' or 'comparison'
Changed the first vowel from 'a' to 'i' and added an alif to make it mean 'an example of something'
Changed to plural form by adding hamza at start and changing internal vowels
Added the definite article ال to specify these particular examples
Added fatha at end to show this word is the object of the verb تَضْرِبُوا (to make/set forth)
This is a special emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain. It's like adding 'definitely' or 'certainly' in English.
The shadda (ّ) on the ن shows extra strength in the meaning, making the emphasis even stronger
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل, making this the unique name for The One God

steps.m: Allah (as the object being compared to in this ayah)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a special mark (ٱ) at the start and a double L sound (ّ). The ending mark (َ) shows it's being compared to something else in this verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on middle letter, and adding ُ at end
Added م at the end to make it refer to multiple people instead of just one person
Added damma (ُ) and sukun (ْ) marks to show correct pronunciation
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Basic negative word that makes the verb تَعْلَمُونَ (you know) mean its opposite (you do not know)
Added fatha (َ) on ل to show how to pronounce it correctly
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense

steps.m: you (all) know (in response to لَا 'do not')
steps.n: Added ونَ at the end to show that many people are being addressed. This verb comes after لَا (do not) in the ayah, showing a prohibition.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make past tense. In this ayah, Allah is presenting a parable, so we use the meaning 'presented' rather than the literal 'struck'

This is a unique word that refers specifically to The One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the' in English) and the word underwent special changes to become this specific divine name.

steps.m: Allah (The One True God who sets forth this example)
steps.n: Added the shadda to emphasize the ل, changed the initial alif to a waṣla (ٱ), and added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action of ضَرَبَ (setting forth) in this ayah
Added fathas to connect the letters and form the noun 'example'

steps.m: an example (object of ضَرَبَ - Allah sets forth)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object receiving the action of ضَرَبَ (sets forth)

steps.m: to serve/be devoted to someone or something

steps.n: Added fatha and sukun to make the basic noun form. This word is being used as an example in Allah's parable.

steps.m: a servant (as an example in the parable)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is an indefinite noun being used as an object. The word describes one of the examples Allah is comparing in this parable.

steps.m: to own or have power over something

steps.n: Changed from a basic word meaning 'own' to showing someone who is owned (like a slave), by adding مَ at start and making middle letter و

steps.m: a slave (object of ضَرَبَ اللَّهُ مَثَلًا)
steps.n: Added shadda for emphasis and tanween to show it's indefinite. This word is being used as an example in Allah's parable.
Shows this is any slave (not a specific one) who is being acted upon
Basic negative particle that denies or negates what comes after it. In this ayah, it negates يَقْدِرُ (able to)
Adding shadda makes the negation stronger, emphasizing that the slave has absolutely no power
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changes from past to present by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel

steps.m: he is able (subject of لَّا in لَّا يَقْدِرُ)
steps.n: Added a damma (u sound) at the end to show this is a complete present tense verb meaning 'he is not able'
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or higher than something else. It's like saying 'on top of' or 'over' in English.
Added the vowel marks to show how to pronounce it correctly - 'a' sound after ع and after ل
Added the small alif (ٰ) at the end to show this is the final form. In this ayah, it's used with شَىْءٍ to show something that has no power or ability.

steps.m: to be a thing, to exist

steps.n: Root letters combined to make the common noun meaning 'thing'
The letter ي is written as ى when it comes in the middle of a word in this position
Added tanwin kasra to show this is indefinite (like 'a thing') and comes after the word عَلَىٰ (upon/to) as the object that يَقْدِرُ (he is able) acts on
This is a compound word made up of و (and) + من (who/whoever)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Added shadda to emphasize the action
Added نا to show 'we' did the action
Added هُ to show who received the provision
This is a basic preposition that shows something coming from somewhere or someone
Added نا at the end to show 'us'. The ن gets doubled (with shadda) when combining with نا because two similar letters come together
Changes from action to the thing itself (like 'provide' to 'provision')
Changes from action (provide) to thing (provision). This word is what is being provided in the phrase رَزَقْنٰهُ (We provided him).

steps.m: a provision (object of رَزَقْنٰهُ - We provided)
steps.n: Adding ًا shows this is what is being provided in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic adjective form
Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this word is describing the provision (رِزْقًا) that Allah gave. Both words end in ًا to show they go together.
Combination of conjunction ف (so/then) with pronoun هو (he)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added يُ at start and changed middle vowel to ِ to make it present tense
Added ُ at the end to show this is the doer/subject of the action (the one who spends)
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added the pronoun هُ (him/it) to show what/who is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to the رِزْقًا (provision) that was mentioned.
Added kasra on first letter and doubled the middle letter to make it into a noun meaning 'secret'
Added tanween alif (ًا) at the end to show this describes how the action of spending is done in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create a noun meaning the act of doing something openly
Added و at beginning to connect this with the previous word سِرًّا (secretly), showing contrast between doing things secretly and openly

steps.m: and openly (describing how يُنفِقُ/he spends is done)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to make it describe how the action is done. This word pairs with سِرًّا to show he spends both secretly and openly.
This is a question word that asks yes/no questions. In this ayah, it's asking if two groups are equal - those who can't do anything and those who spend from what Allah gave them.
Adding vowel marks to make it a past tense verb showing a state
Added است at the beginning to show doing something completely/thoroughly
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing the ending vowel
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to turn the action into a noun meaning the act of praising

steps.m: the praise (subject of this part of the ayah)
steps.n: Added 'the' at the beginning and a mark showing this word is the main topic being discussed
Added لِ at the beginning to show the praise belongs to or is for Allah. Added ِ at the end because it follows الحَمْدُ (praise). This word tells us who the praise is for.
This is a particle that introduces a contrast with what came before. Here it contrasts with the previous question 'Are they equal?' to emphasize that most people don't know the truth.
Basic past tense verb pattern
Added vowels to make it mean 'it became many/abundant' in the past
Added أ at beginning to make comparative form, meaning 'more' or 'most'
Added هُمْ at the end to show this refers to 'them'
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَقْدِرُ (he is able) into 'he is not able'. It's like adding 'not' before a verb in English.
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show present tense

steps.m: they know (referring to most of them/أَكْثَرُهُمْ in the ayah)
steps.n: The suffix ون shows multiple people (they) are doing the action. This refers back to 'most of them' (أَكْثَرُهُمْ) in the ayah.
Connected letters with fatha marks to show this is a complete past-tense verb. This word is connected to مَثَلًا (example) in this ayah.
Added و at beginning to connect this to what came before. Allah is presenting another example after previous examples.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
This is the unique name for The One True God in Islam. The ال makes it specific, the shadda emphasizes it, and the ُ shows it's the subject of the sentence.

steps.m: to be like or resemble something

steps.n: Adding vowels changes this from a root into a proper word meaning 'example'
Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this is an indefinite noun being acted upon. Allah is setting forth an example in this ayah.

steps.m: to be a man, to walk like a man

steps.n: The vowels make it a noun meaning 'man'
Added يْنِ at the end to show exactly two men (dual form). The doubled ر (with shaddah) shows it connects smoothly to the word before it. This word is being compared in Allah's example.
Connected the letters with fatha marks to form the basic word
Added damma to show this is the subject doing something
Added هُمَا to show this refers to 'one of them two'
Added ا to show this refers to both of them
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: mute, speechless (describing رَّجُلَيْنِ أَحَدُهُمَآ - one of two men)
steps.n: Changed to an adjective describing someone who cannot speak. This describes one of the two men mentioned in the ayah.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَقْدِرُ (he is able) into 'he is not able'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the past tense verb
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This word comes after لا (not) in the ayah.
Adds ُ at the end to show this is the main subject doing the action, even though it's negated by لا
This is a preposition showing something is on top of, over, or dependent on something else. In this ayah, it shows how the mute person (أَبْكَمُ) is dependent on his guardian/master (مَوْلَىٰهُ).
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to make it into a noun meaning 'thing'
Added tanwin kasra at the end because it comes after the word عَلَىٰ (upon/on). The ي is written as ى when it has a sukun.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation: damma (ُ) on ه and fatha (َ) on و

steps.m: and he (subject of يَسْتَوِى - is equal)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word is one of the subjects being compared in يَسْتَوِى (is equal).
Doubling the middle letter with shadda makes the meaning stronger

steps.m: a complete burden (describing the person in the parable)
steps.n: The double damma shows this is describing someone who is a complete burden on others, referring to the person mentioned in this parable who cannot do anything for himself
This is a basic preposition that shows something is positioned on top of, upon, or over something else. In this ayah, it shows what the person cannot do - they cannot do/manage anything.
The small alif (ىٰ) at the end is just a writing style that doesn't change the meaning. It shows this is the end of the word when it's written.

steps.m: to be close to or protect

steps.n: Added مَ at the start and ى at the end to make it mean 'the one who protects' or 'the one who is protected' (can mean either)
Added هُ at the end to show it belongs to 'him'. This word is connected to كَلٌّ (burden) in the phrase 'a burden upon his master'.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation of the question word 'where'
Added ما at the end to change 'where' into 'wherever'. This word connects to يُوَجِّههُّ (he directs him) to show that the direction can be in any place.

steps.m: to direct or guide something/someone towards a direction

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the action more intense or done multiple times
Adding يُ changes it from past to present tense
Changed from passive (being directed) to active (directing) and added هُ (him) to show who is being directed
Added shadda on the final هُ to add emphasis
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَقْدِرُ (he is able) negative, changing its meaning to 'he is not able'
Added يَ at the beginning to show 'he' is doing the action in the present/future
The ي at the end is dropped because it comes after لا (meaning 'not'). This is like saying 'does not come' in English.
Added vowels to make the noun form meaning 'good' or 'goodness'. This word is what's being brought/delivered in the phrase لَا يَأْتِ بِخَيْرٍ (does not bring good).

steps.m: with good/goodness (object of يَأْتِ - he brings)
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to mean 'with' and tanween at end to show it's indefinite (any good/goodness). The whole phrase لَا يَأْتِ بِخَيْرٍ means 'he does not bring any good'.

steps.m: is it? (asking about يَسْتَوِى - are they equal)
steps.n: This is a question word that changes a statement into a yes/no question. Here it's asking if two people are equal - the one who can't do anything and the one who commands justice.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Adding ي at start changes it from past to present tense
This is a pronoun that refers to the person who commands with justice, mentioned in the next part of the ayah. It's connected to the verb يَأْمُرُ (he commands/orders).
This is a question word that asks about people, like 'who' in English
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it in the ayah
This is the basic concept of commanding or ordering

steps.m: command! (as an order to one person)
steps.n: Changed from base word to a command by adding damma (u sound) at the end - like saying 'do it!'
Added يَ at the start to change from 'command!' to 'he commands/is commanding', and added dots to show how to pronounce it clearly
Added final damma to show this is the doer of the action. This word refers back to هُوَ (he) in the ayah.

steps.m: to be balanced, fair, or equal

steps.n: Changes the basic idea of being fair into the noun 'justice' - like how 'fair' becomes 'fairness'
Adding ال makes it talk about a specific kind of justice, not just justice in general

steps.m: with/by the justice (object of يَأْمُرُ - commands)
steps.n: Adds بِ at the start to show this justice is being used as a tool or method. This connects to the verb يَأْمُرُ (commands) in the verse, showing HOW the commanding is done - it's done with justice.

steps.m: with/by the justice (object of يَأْمُرُ - commands)
steps.n: The ِ (kasra) at the end shows this word is affected by the word 'with' (بِ) that comes before it
This is a pronoun meaning 'he' that refers back to the person being described (أَحَدُهُمَآ أَبْكَمُ - one of them who is mute). It's the subject of كَلٌّ (burden).
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
This is a basic preposition showing position or relationship. The ى at the end makes it work like 'on' or 'upon' in English.
The small alif (ٰ) above the ى is just a pronunciation helper - it doesn't change the meaning. In this ayah, it shows what يَقْدِرُ (he is able) relates to شَىْءٍ (anything).
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together
Added alif (ٰ) after ر which is a common spelling variation in the Quran
Added tanween kasra to show this is indefinite (like 'a path') and comes after the word على (on/upon)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (he stood) to describe someone who stands/is straight
Added مُسْتَ at the beginning to make it mean 'completely/firmly straight' - like upgrading from standing to being perfectly upright
Added shadda on م and tanween at the end to show this describes the path mentioned in the ayah
This is the base word referring to God in Arabic
Added ل at the beginning to show something belongs or relates to Allah
Added vowel marks to show proper pronunciation and grammatical role

steps.m: and to/for Allah (connected to what follows: غَيْبُ ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ - the unseen of the heavens)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what follows in the verse about the unseen of the heavens
Transforms into a noun meaning 'unseen/hidden realm'. This word is in the nominative case and is possessed by Allah (لله) in a possession relationship.
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject being possessed by Allah in this ayah
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Added hamza to create noun meaning 'sky'
Added feminine plural ending, with hamza changing to و
Added 'the' (ال) at the start, which makes the س double-strength (with shadda)
Changed to 'of the heavens' by adding the -i ending, showing it belongs to غَيْبُ (unseen)
Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make it a noun
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific earth/ground rather than any earth/ground
وَ means 'and', ٱلْ makes it specific ('the earth'), and ِ shows it's affected by فِى
This is a question word that asks 'what'. The fatha helps with pronunciation.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
The alif is elongated (آ) to help with pronunciation, but doesn't change the meaning
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning the result of commanding
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject being discussed in relation to السَّاعَةِ (the Hour)
Added an alif (ا) between س and ع, and vowels, to make it mean 'it flowed' in the past
Added ة to make it refer to one specific instance of time passing

steps.m: the Hour (referring to the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda because it comes after ال

steps.m: of the Hour (referring to the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word أَمْرُ (matter/affair of)
This is a basic particle used for making exceptions. It's not derived from a root.

Adding shadda and alif makes this a complete exception particle. In this ayah, it connects 'like a glance' (كَلَمْحِ) with 'the matter' (أَمْرُ), showing that the matter is nothing except like a glance.
Changed from an action (looking) to the name of that action (a look)

steps.m: like a quick look (connected to البَصَرِ - the glance of the eye)
steps.n: Added كَ at the start to mean 'like' and ِ at the end to connect it to the next word (البَصَرِ) which means 'of the eye'
Added vowel marks to transform the root into a noun meaning the ability or act of seeing
Added 'the' at the beginning and a mark at the end to show this sight belongs to or is connected to something else

This is a basic connecting word that gives a choice between two options. In this ayah, it connects 'like a glance' with 'closer', showing the Day of Judgment will happen as quick as a blink or even quicker.

steps.m: he/it (referring to أَمْرُ ٱلسَّاعَةِ - the matter of the Hour)
steps.n: This is a pronoun that refers back to 'the matter of the Hour' mentioned earlier in the ayah. It's used to refer to a masculine singular thing or person.
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb meaning 'was near'
Added أَ at beginning and changed vowels to make comparative form meaning 'nearer/closer'

This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it. The shadda (ّ) makes the ن sound stronger, which helps show this emphasis. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah is capable of all things.
Changed to the unique form used only for Allah, with ال merged into the word itself

steps.m: Allah (as the object being referred to in إِنَّ)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a special mark (shadda) on the ل and a mark at the end (fatha) because it comes after the word إِنَّ (indeed)
This is the basic form meaning something high or elevated
Adding ى changes this into a preposition showing position or authority over something

steps.m: upon, on, over (relating to كُلِّ شَىْءٍ - every thing)
steps.n: The small alif (ٰ) is added as a pronunciation guide to show the long vowel sound. This word connects to كُلِّ شَىْءٍ (every thing) in the ayah to show Allah's power over all things.
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because this word is describing شَىْءٍ (thing) in the phrase 'of every thing'
Adding vowels to make it readable and pronounceable
Added the genitive case ending (tanween kasra) because this word follows كُلِّ (every/all) in the ayah. Also changed the ي to ى which is a common spelling variation.

steps.m: to determine, measure, have power over

steps.n: Forms an intensive adjective showing complete and perfect power. In this ayah, it describes Allah's absolute power over everything.

steps.m: All-Powerful, having absolute power (describes Allah's perfect power over everything mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: The tanween shows this is a characteristic of Allah mentioned after إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ (Indeed, Allah over all things...)
This is a unique proper name that doesn't come from a root. It's the personal name of God in Islam.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence

steps.m: and God (as the subject/doer of أَخْرَجَكُم - brought you out)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement. Allah is the one who brought you out (أَخْرَجَكُم) in this verse.
Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
Adding أَ at the beginning changes the meaning from doing something yourself to making someone else do it

steps.m: he made you all exit (Allah made you exit from your mothers' wombs)
steps.n: Added كُم at the end to show who was made to exit. In this ayah, Allah is talking about bringing us out from our mothers' wombs when we were born.
This is the basic preposition that shows something moving away from somewhere or coming from a source
Added kasra under first letter to show how it's pronounced
Added shadda to emphasize the م sound. This happens because the word comes after a word ending in ن or م
Added a small noon at the end to show a slight nasal sound when connecting to the next word (بُطُونِ)
Added short vowels (fat-ha and sukoon) to make the basic noun meaning 'belly'
Changed vowels to damma and added ون to make it plural
Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to or is connected to 'your mothers' that comes after it

steps.m: to be a source or origin

steps.n: Added damma and doubled the middle letter to create the noun for mother
Added هٰت to form the irregular plural. The ٰ (alif) shows elongation of the vowel.
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by what comes after it
Added كُمْ to show the mothers belong to 'you all' (plural)
This is the basic negative particle that makes the verb after it negative, like adding 'not' in English. Here it makes تَعْلَمُونَ (you know) into 'you do not know'
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Prefix تَ changes verb from 3rd to 2nd person

steps.m: you (all) know (referring to the people being addressed in this ayah about what they know when they are born)
steps.n: Changed from 'you know' (singular) to 'you all know' (plural) by adding ون at the end
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to make a noun. This word is what's being known (the object) in the phrase 'you do not know anything'.
Added tanween fatha (ًا) at the end to show this is the object being acted upon. In this context, it means 'anything' because it's used with لا تَعْلَمُونَ (you do not know).

steps.m: to make or put something in a certain way
he made/put (something for لَكُمُ - for you)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fathas to show this happened in the past. This verb is connected to لَكُمُ (for you) in this ayah, showing Allah made something for people.

steps.m: and he made/put (something for لَكُمُ - for you)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this action with the previous statement about Allah bringing people out from their mothers' wombs
This is a particle that shows something is being given or directed to someone
Added كُمُ to show this is being done for multiple people. This word is receiving the action of جَعَلَ (He made) in this ayah.

steps.m: to hear or listen to something

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to hear' into the noun 'hearing'
Added 'the' at beginning and fatha at end because this word is the object (thing receiving the action) of جَعَلَ (He made/placed)

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and make a basic noun for the ability to see
Changes singular 'sight' into plural 'sights' using a common plural pattern
Makes it specific by adding 'the'
Adds 'and' to connect with previous word. The ending َ shows this is the object of the verb جَعَلَ (He made/placed)
The pattern makes it a noun referring to the physical/spiritual heart
Changes to broken plural form for small number of hearts
Adding ال makes it specific hearts rather than just any hearts
Added و (and) at start and َ at end to show this is one of the things Allah gave you, along with hearing and sight
Added vowel marks and shadda to show correct pronunciation. This word shows hope or expectation about something that might happen.
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being addressed. This word connects to تَشْكُرُونَ (you give thanks) in the ayah, showing hope that they will be thankful.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
The ُ (damma) shows this is about being grateful by nature, not just a one-time action
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added ونَ at the end to show many people are doing the action
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of verbs to 'did not' in the past
Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a question that expects the answer 'yes'. In this ayah, it's asking 'Did they not see?' expecting the answer 'Yes, they did see.'
Added vowels (fatha) to make the past tense verb 'he saw'
Added يَ at start to show present tense. The root letters أ and ي merge into ى because they are soft letters that combine together
Added وْا at the end to show multiple people doing the action
This is a basic preposition that shows direction. In this ayah, it connects 'Do they not see' with 'the birds', showing what they should look towards.

steps.m: to fly or soar through the air

steps.n: Changed from action (flying) to thing that does the action (bird)
Added emphasis on first letter to help pronunciation after adding ال
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it comes after the word إِلَى (to/towards) in the ayah

steps.m: to control or make something serve a purpose

steps.n: Doubling of خ shows this is complete control, not just basic control
The مُ at the start shows this is happening to something rather than something doing the action. In this ayah, Allah is the one doing the controlling.
Added alif maksura (ٰ) to help with pronunciation
Added ات with tanween to show this refers to multiple things being controlled (in this case, the birds mentioned as الطَّيْرِ)

steps.m: in (connects جَوِّ to what comes before it)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows location or containment. Here it connects 'the birds' to their location 'in the atmosphere of the sky'
Adding the fatha mark shows this is a noun

steps.m: of the atmosphere (describing where الطَّيْرِ/birds are)
steps.n: The word gets a kasra (i) sound at the end because it comes after the word 'in' (فِي), showing where the birds are flying

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high up
Added ٱل to show we're talking about the specific sky above us, and doubled the س (shown by ّ) because it comes after ٱل

steps.m: of the sky (in جَوِّ ٱلسَّمَآءِ - the atmosphere of the sky)
steps.n: Added ِ at the end to show this word is connected to جَوِّ (atmosphere) that comes before it in the ayah
This is a negative particle that means 'nothing/no one' in this context. It's connected to the verb يُمْسِكُهُنَّ (holds them), creating the meaning 'nothing/no one holds them'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changes from past to present tense by adding يُ at the start and changing middle vowel to ِ
Added masculine singular object pronoun
Added نَّ to make the object feminine plural
This is a basic exception particle that shows something is being excluded. It doesn't come from a root word.
Added shadda and alif to complete the exception particle. In this ayah, it excludes everything except Allah (اللَّهُ) from being able to hold up the birds.
Added ال to specify this is THE one true deity, and the first letter merged with the ل

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of يُمْسِكُهُنَّ - holds them)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل to emphasize it, changed initial alif to hamzat wasl, and added damma to show this is the doer of the action (holding the birds) in this ayah

Adding the shadda (ّ) changes this from a conditional word into an emphasis word. It makes what comes after it stronger, like adding an exclamation mark in English. Here it emphasizes that the signs (ءَايٰتٍ) are definitely real.
This is a basic preposition that shows location. In this ayah, it shows that the birds are in the expanse of the sky.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (overbar) to show it's pointing to something far away instead of nearby

steps.m: that, that one (pointing to something far)
steps.n: Adding لِكَ makes it more specific and emphatic, like saying 'that one' instead of just 'that'

steps.m: to show, make clear, be evident

steps.n: Adds the basic vowel mark to create the singular noun 'sign'
Makes the word indefinite and shows it's in the genitive case
Added ل to show these are specific signs being referred to

steps.m: surely there are signs (for قَوْمٍ - a people)
steps.n: The letter lam (ل) at the start means 'surely/indeed', and the tanwin kasra (ٍ) at the end shows it connects to 'people' (قَوْمٍ) that comes after it

steps.m: to stand together as a group

steps.n: Root meaning 'stand together' becomes a noun meaning 'people/nation' by adding vowels (fatha on ق and sukun on و)
Adds emphatic preposition prefix meaning 'for/to' with shadda for emphasis
Adds tanween kasra to show this is indefinite. This word relates to يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) which comes after it, showing these are signs specifically for people who believe.

steps.m: to make someone feel secure/to believe
steps.n: Adding long alif at start changes meaning to 'causing security/belief'
