Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. The hamza appears because it started with alif
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the believing
This is a unique word that refers specifically to the one true God in Islam. While it comes from the root meaning 'deity', it has become a proper name.
Added shadda to show that the ل is doubled in pronunciation
Added damma to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence. Allah is the one who 'made' (جَعَلَ) things for people.

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of جَعَلَ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this sentence with what came before it, like saying 'and'. Also added the helper mark (ٱ) that shows how to pronounce the ال correctly after و.

steps.m: to make something into something else
he made (something into something for you all)
steps.n: Basic past tense form showing Allah made something into something else, specifically for the benefit of people (connected to لَكُم which means 'for you all')
Starting with the basic particle that shows direction or purpose
Added the plural 'you' ending to show who the action is for. This word is the indirect object of جَعَلَ (He made) in this ayah, showing who Allah made things for.
Basic preposition meaning 'from' - used to show where something comes from or what it's made of
Added shadda (ّ) on م because it comes before ب in بُيُوتِكُمْ. This is a pronunciation rule to make it flow better. Also added a small ن mark (ۢ) to show the word connects to what comes after it.
Changes from the root meaning 'house' to the plural form 'houses' by adding vowel marks and و in the middle
Adds كُمْ to show the houses belong to multiple people (you all), with ِ connecting it to the word

steps.m: to dwell, to rest, to be calm

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: a dwelling place (object of جَعَلَ - made/placed)
steps.n: Changed to a noun meaning 'dwelling place' with tanween fatha showing it's the object of جَعَلَ (Allah made)

steps.m: to make something into something else, to put, to set
he made/put (connected to جَعَلَ earlier in verse)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb. This is the second instance of جَعَلَ in this verse, connecting back to the first one.
The و (meaning 'and') was added at the start to connect this action to what came before it
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added كُم at the end to show this is for multiple people being addressed. This word is the indirect object of جَعَلَ (He made) in this ayah, showing who something was made for.

Connected the root letters together to form the singular noun for one skin
Changed the middle and end vowels and added و to make it plural (like book → books)
Added kasra to show this word owns or belongs to the word after it (الْأَنْعٰمِ - the livestock)
Added vowels to form the basic noun for livestock - animals that provide benefits to people
Changes from single animal to plural form meaning many animals
Adds 'the' to specify these particular cattle and shows this word belongs to جُلُودِ (skins/hides) in the phrase 'from the skins/hides of the cattle'
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'house'
Makes the singular 'house' into plural 'houses' by changing inside vowels and adding و
Shows this word is receiving the action of جَعَلَ (made/appointed)
Basic past tense verb pattern with vowels added

steps.m: to consider/find something to be light
steps.n: Adding است at beginning shows you're testing or evaluating the lightness of something
Adds تَ at start to make it 'you' in present tense

The هَا (ha) at the end shows what thing is being considered light - like saying 'it' in English
Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'day'
Added a fatha mark at the end to show this word is in the accusative case
Changes the root into a noun meaning 'one instance of traveling'
Adds kasra at the end to show this word is the object of a preposition

steps.m: your (plural) traveling (in relation to يَوْمَ - on the day of)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this belongs to multiple people being addressed. This word comes after يَوْمَ (day of) in the ayah.
Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make the noun for 'day'
Added fatha at the end to show this word answers 'when?' about staying (إِقَامَتِكُمْ) mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: and on (the) day (when you stay - إِقَامَتِكُمْ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous phrase about when you travel (يَوْمَ ظَعْنِكُمْ)

steps.m: to establish, set up, make firm

steps.n: Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding 'aa' sound in middle
Added hamza at start to make causative form
Changed to noun form showing the act itself
Added 'ت' and kasra at the end to show it belongs to something (like saying 'residence of')
Added plural 'your' ending
This is a preposition that shows something comes from somewhere. Here it connects to 'their wool' (أَصْوَافِهَا) to show what something is made from
Adds 'and' to connect it with what came before
Added vowels to form basic noun meaning 'wool'
Made plural by adding أ at the start and ا after و - this is a common plural pattern for some nouns
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something
Added هَا to show the wools belong to the livestock mentioned earlier
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a basic noun
Changed to broken plural pattern أَفْعَال to show multiple furs
Added feminine possessive pronoun ها to show these furs belong to the livestock mentioned earlier
Added conjunction و to connect this word to the previous items in the list
Added short vowels (fat-ha and sukoon) to the root letters to make the word mean 'a piece of hair'
Changes to broken plural form to show many pieces of hair/fur
Adds conjunction 'and' to connect with previous items in the list (wool and fur)
Adds kasra to show possession

steps.m: and their hairs/furs (referring to the cattle)
steps.n: Adds feminine possessive pronoun referring back to the cattle mentioned earlier

steps.m: to furnish, to fill with goods

steps.n: Forms a noun meaning furniture. This is what Allah made/provided (جَعَلَ) for people according to the ayah.
Adds tanween fatha to show this is the object being provided. The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this Quranic spelling.
Changed from basic root letters to a word meaning 'benefit/enjoyment' by adding short vowel marks (fat-ha)
Added alif to show it's a thing that results from the action of providing benefit
Added tanween to show it's indefinite and being acted upon in the sentence
Added و to connect this word to the previous items Allah has provided as benefits
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or extent. Here it connects with حِينٍ (a time) to mean 'to/until a time'
Added an alif khanjariyah (ىٰ) at the end which is just a writing style variation - it doesn't change the meaning
Root letters for 'time' got vowel marks to make it a proper noun meaning 'a period of time'
Added tanwin (two kasras) at the end to show this is any time/period, not a specific one
This is a unique proper name that doesn't come from a root. It's the personal name of God in Islam.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence. Allah is the one who 'made' (جَعَلَ) things for people in this verse.

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of جَعَلَ/made)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this to the previous verse, and added the helping alif (ٱ) that comes with ال

steps.m: he made/placed (connected to لَكُم - for you)
steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This verb is connected to لَكُم which shows who it was made for.
Added fatha to show the basic meaning of direction or purpose

steps.m: for you all (object of جَعَلَ - He made)
steps.n: Added كُم to show this is directed to multiple people. This word shows who receives what Allah made in this verse.

steps.m: from what (connected to خَلَقَ - He created)
steps.n: Combined two words: مِن meaning 'from' and ما meaning 'what'. This combination asks about what Allah created.
When مِن comes before a word starting with م, the ن changes into another م with a shadda (doubled letter mark) to make it easier to say

steps.m: to create or form something from nothing

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word is connected to جَعَلَ (he made) in this ayah, showing Allah's actions.
Changed the root into a plural noun showing multiple shades or sheltering things

steps.m: shades/shelters (as something Allah provides for you)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this is what Allah provides (object of جَعَلَ meaning 'He made/provided')

steps.m: to make something into something else, to put, to place
he made/placed (connected to لَكُم - for you)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to show this happened in the past. This verb is connected to لَكُم which shows who it was done for.
Conjunction و added to connect with previous statement
Added fatha to show the vowel sound on the preposition ل

steps.m: for you all (object of جَعَلَ - He made)
steps.n: Added the plural pronoun كم meaning 'you all'. This word shows who receives the action of Allah making things in this verse.
Basic preposition showing source or origin
Added emphasis by doubling the م with a shadda
Added fatha at the end to connect with the next word الْجِبَالِ (the mountains)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Changed vowel pattern and added alif to show many mountains

steps.m: the mountains (source of shelter mentioned in أَكْنٰنًا)
steps.n: Adds ال to make it definite and kasra at the end because it comes after مِنَ (from) in the ayah
Added أ at start and ا after second letter to make it into a plural noun for places where the action happens

steps.m: places of shelter (object of جَعَلَ - He made)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this word is receiving the action of جَعَلَ (Allah made these shelters)

steps.m: to make something into something else, to put, to set
he made/put (connected to لَكُم - for you)
steps.n: Connected the three root letters with fatha vowels to show this happened in the past. This verb is connected to لَكُم which shows who it was made for.

steps.m: and he made/put (connected to لَكُم - for you)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement about what Allah made for people
Added كُمْ to show who the action is for. This word is connected to جَعَلَ (He made), showing who He made things for.
Changed to a plural form that shows multiple pieces of clothing
Changed the alif to an alif maksura (ٰ) and added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb تَقِيكُم (protects you)

steps.m: to protect or guard from harm

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra
Changed from 'he protects' to 'she protects' by replacing يَ with تَ at start

steps.m: it protects you all (referring to how سَرٰبِيلَ - garments protect)
steps.n: Added كُمُ to show who is being protected (you all)

steps.n: The doubled ر with shadda shows this isn't just regular warmth, but intense heat. This word is the object of تَقِيكُمُ (protects you from) in this ayah.

steps.m: the intense heat (that Allah protects you from)
steps.n: Added ال to show this is talking about the specific heat mentioned as something Allah protects from. The َ at the end shows this heat is being acted upon by the protection.
Root letters form a noun meaning 'garment' by adding vowels
Changes from one garment to many garments by adding extra letters to make it plural
The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) for Quranic writing style
Adds conjunction و and final fatha to show this is the object of the verb جَعَلَ (He made/provided)

steps.m: to protect or shield from harm

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Changed to present tense feminine form since سَرٰبِيلَ (garments) is feminine. The weak letter ى changes to ي in present tense.

Changed into a noun showing the concept of might or strength. This word is what the garments protect you from in تَقِيكُم (protect you from).
Added كُمْ at the end to show it belongs to multiple people ('your' plural). The َ (fatha) before كُمْ shows this word receives the action of protection.
Starting with the basic demonstrative word ذا (this). The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) in this case.
Adding لِ changes the meaning from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far)
Adding كَ at the end completes the demonstrative word 'that'

steps.m: like that, likewise (connects to يُتِمُّ - He completes)

Added shadda to show emphasis and combine repeated letters
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. This word connects to نِعْمَتَهُۥ (His favor) which comes after it in the ayah.
Root letters joined together to make a simple word meaning 'blessing'
Changed from general 'blessing' to a specific one by adding ة at the end and changing the vowel mark

When adding a pronoun after عَلَى, the ى changes to يْ to make it easier to connect
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to

Changed from 'he' to 'you' by adding تُ at start and changing vowels to show present tense
Added plural ending ون to show multiple people are addressed

Added تَ at beginning to show doing action to oneself
Added وْا at the end to show multiple people did this action

Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before (فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا۟ - 'if they turn away')

Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. In this ayah, they are recognizing نِعْمَتَ ٱللَّهِ (Allah's favor).

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing the action

Added أ at start and changed vowels to make comparative form
Added ُ to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added هُمُ to show we're talking about 'them'
Added وَ at start to connect this with previous phrase

Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb نَبْعَثُ (we raise/resurrect)
Added و with fatha at beginning to connect this to the previous statement

steps.m: a witness (object of نَبْعَثُ - we raise)

This is the basic form of the word that shows something happens after something else
The shadda with fatha at the end makes the meaning stronger and more definite
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يُؤْذَنُ (they will be permitted) into 'they will not be permitted'
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Basic past tense verb meaning 'to permit'

This is the basic demonstrative word that points to someone or something specific
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one
Adding لِ at the beginning shows this is 'for' or 'to' those people. The ّ (shadda) appears because لِ combines with ال. This word connects to كَفَرُوا (they disbelieved) which comes right after it.

steps.m: they rejected/hid truth (subject of لَا يُؤْذَنُ)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action. The ُ (damma) before وا is needed for proper pronunciation.
The small circle (۟) is added above وا to show it's a plural marker
This is a negative particle that makes the following verb يُسْتَعْتَبُونَ negative, meaning they will not be allowed to make amends
The و connects this negation to the previous statement, showing these things happen in sequence - first they won't be given permission, and then they won't be allowed to make amends
This is a standalone pronoun meaning 'they' or 'them' when talking about a group of males or mixed group. In this ayah, it's connected to the verb يُسْتَعْتَبُونَ (they are allowed to make amends).
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show asking or seeking something

steps.m: he is asked to make amends
steps.n: Changed from 'he asked' to 'he is asked' by adding يُ at start and ُ at end to make it passive (something done to him)

steps.m: they are asked to make amends
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are involved

steps.m: when (introduces what happens at a specific time)
steps.n: This is a word that introduces what happens at a specific time, like saying 'at the time when' or 'whenever' in English. In this ayah, it introduces what happens when the wrongdoers see their punishment.
The و connects this phrase to what came before it in the Quran, showing this is part of a sequence of events

steps.m: to see or understand something with eyes or mind

steps.n: Added fathas to make it past tense. This word is connected to يُخَفَّفُ (it is lightened) in the ayah, showing who saw.
The ي at the end changes to ا, and the أ changes to ء because of spelling rules for weak letters. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is the basic form of the word that connects descriptions to nouns, like saying 'the person who ran' or 'the book that I read'
Adding ال and doubling the ذ makes it point to a specific person or thing
Adding ن at the end makes it refer to multiple people. In this ayah, it refers to the people who did wrong (ظَلَمُوا)

steps.m: to wrong or be unjust to someone

steps.n: Added fatha marks to connect the letters and show this is about something that already happened
The ending وا shows many people (they) did this action. This word is connected to رَءَا (saw) in the ayah - it tells us who saw.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes verb into noun meaning 'punishment'. This is what the wrongdoers see in the ayah.

steps.m: the punishment (what is seen in رَءَا)
steps.n: Adds definite article to make the noun definite

steps.m: the punishment (object of رَءَا - they saw)
steps.n: Gets -a ending because it's what they saw (object)
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يُخَفَّفُ (lightened/reduced) that comes after it in the ayah
The فَ shows this negation is a consequence of what came before - when the wrongdoers see the punishment, as a result it will not be lightened for them
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on middle letter makes the action more intense

steps.m: it is being lightened (العَذَابَ - the punishment)
steps.n: Adding يُ at start changes it to present tense passive voice, showing the punishment is being acted upon

steps.m: it is being lightened (العَذَابَ - the punishment)
steps.n: The damma ending shows this is happening now (present tense) - it's the main action in the sentence

steps.m: from, away from (preposition related to يُخَفَّفُ)
steps.n: This is a preposition showing movement away from something. In this ayah, it connects with يُخَفَّفُ (lightened/reduced) to show what is not being reduced from them.
هُمْ means 'them' (referring to ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ - those who wronged). This shows who the punishment won't be lightened from.
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يُنظَرُونَ (they are given respite) that comes after it
The و connects this negative with the previous negative phrase فَلَا (then not), creating a sequence of two negations
This is a basic pronoun that refers to a group of males or a mixed group. In this ayah, it's connected to the verb يُنظَرُونَ (they are given respite), showing who is not being given respite.

steps.m: to look at, to wait for

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he looked' to 'he is given time/delay' by adding yu- at start and changing vowels to make it passive. Like changing from doing to having something done to you.
Added ون to show this happens to multiple people

steps.m: when (introduces the time when the disbelievers saw their partners)
steps.n: This is a time word that introduces when something happened. In this ayah, it introduces when the disbelievers saw their false gods/partners.

steps.m: and when (connects to previous ayah and introduces the time when the disbelievers saw their partners)
steps.n: The و connects this new scene or event to what happened in the previous ayah, showing this is part of a sequence of events
The root letters combine to mean the act of seeing
Basic root letters got vowels (fat-ha marks) to make 'he saw' in past tense
The ي at the end changes to ا, and the أ changes to ء because of rules about weak letters. This word is connected to قَالُوا۟ (they said) in this ayah - it shows what happened before they spoke.
This is a relative pronoun used to describe a group of people. The base word ذِي takes the definite article ال and plural suffix ين
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning and changing the first vowel to sukun makes the verb causative - meaning to make something happen
Adding وا at the end makes the verb refer to a group of people (they) instead of just one person (he)

steps.m: to share or associate with others
steps.n: This is the basic concept of sharing or partnering with others
Changes from basic concept to plural noun meaning multiple partners
The word already has آء at the end - this is just a spelling variation of the same plural word meaning 'partners'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: their partners (as object of رَءَا, saw)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show these are their partners. This word is what is being seen (object) in the phrase 'when those who associated partners saw their partners'
Adding the basic past tense vowel pattern

steps.m: they said (referring to those who associated partners with Allah)
steps.n: The word changed from 'he said' to 'they said' by: 1) Flipping the middle letter و to ا, and 2) Adding وا at the end to show many people did the action. In this ayah, it refers to the disbelievers speaking to Allah about their false partners.
Added fatha to first letter and doubled the second letter with shadda to create the noun meaning 'lord'
Added نَا at the end to show it belongs to us (our Lord). This is what's being said in the statement introduced by قَالُوا (they said).
Basic attention-drawing element that starts many demonstrative pronouns
Adds the pointing element that shows we're indicating something specific

steps.m: these (referring to their false partners they used to worship)
steps.n: Adds the plural marker to show we're pointing to multiple things or people. In this ayah, it refers to the false gods/partners the disbelievers used to worship.

steps.m: to join or associate with others

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to associate' into noun meaning 'one who associates/partner'
Changes single partner into multiple partners
Changed to show these partners are doing the action by adding ُ at the end (like saying 'the partners' vs 'the partners are')
Added نَا at the end to show these are 'our' partners. This word is connected to هٰٓؤُلَآءِ (these) in the ayah.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects parts of a sentence
Adding ال with a shadda makes it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ين at the end makes it plural, changing from 'the one who' to 'those who'. This refers to the people who associated partners with Allah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense form
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This word is the subject of نَدْعُوا۟ (we call/invoke) in this ayah.
Connected root letters with fatha marks. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action. Changed vowels to show present tense. The و at the end is the original root letter returning.
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects with دُونِكَ to mean 'from besides you'.

steps.m: to be below or less than something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'below' or 'lesser'
Added kasra to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Added كَ to show this means 'besides you'. This word comes after مِن (from/besides) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أ at start makes it mean 'to cause/make someone do the action'
Adding وا at the end makes it mean multiple people (they) did the action
Adding فَ at start connects this action to what happened before, meaning 'so/then they threw'
This is a compound preposition: إلى (to) + هم (them)
Changed from action to noun by adding fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter
Added ال to make it definite and fatha at end to show it's the object being thrown/cast
Adding shadda changes this from 'if' to 'indeed'. This emphasizes what comes after it.

steps.m: indeed you (plural) (subject of لَكٰذِبُونَ - are liars)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this is addressing multiple people. This word connects to لَكٰذِبُونَ (are liars) in the ayah to mean 'indeed you are liars'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
This form shows who does the lying (active participle)
The masculine sound plural suffix ون is added to indicate multiple male subjects
The لَ prefix adds emphasis, like saying 'truly' or 'indeed'
Changed root letters into past tense 'he' form by adding a-vowels (fatha) between letters
Added أ at the start to make the action more forceful or intense - like throwing something really hard instead of just tossing it
Added وا at the end to show many people did the action
Added و with fatha at start to connect this action with what came before
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or movement towards something. In this ayah, it connects to the word اللَّهِ (Allah) to show movement or direction towards Allah.
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل, changing it from 'a god' to 'The God'

Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word إِلَى (to) before it. The hamza at the start changed to a special connecting hamza (ٱ). The ل is doubled with a shadda to emphasize its importance.
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'day'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon

steps.m: on that day (when they cast to Allah)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ to specify 'that specific day' being discussed in the ayah - refers to when they cast to Allah
Adding vowels makes it a past tense verb
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Doubles the س to emphasize the completeness of the peace/submission
Added basic past tense vowels to show who did the action and when
When two same letters come together at the end, they combine with a shadda (ّ)

steps.m: and he lost his way (connected to أَلْقَوْا - they threw)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this action with the previous one (they threw)
Basic preposition showing separation or topic

steps.m: away from them (referring to their false gods)
steps.n: Added هُم which means 'them' when referring to a group. In this ayah, it refers back to the false gods they used to worship.
This is a basic word that asks about something or refers to something general. Here it refers to the false things they used to make up.
The shadda is added because this word comes after another word ending in a similar sound. It shows what they were inventing/making up.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter). Added past tense vowels.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Form VIII shows doing something deliberately or with effort
Present tense marker indicating ongoing/habitual action

steps.m: they fabricate (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the ayah)
steps.n: Added masculine plural suffix ون to indicate 'they'
Starting with the basic demonstrative word ذا and changing it to ذي which can mean 'who'
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ين at the end makes it plural, changing from 'the one who' to 'those who'. This word refers to the people who disbelieved (كَفَرُوا) in the next part of the verse.
Adding the final vowel marks and changing the first alif to a waṣla alif (ٱ) with a shadda on the lām

steps.m: to reject, deny, or cover up truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb. This word shows what one person did in the past.
Added وا at the end to show many people (all male or mixed group) did this action together in the past. The small mark ۟ above وا is just to help with pronunciation.
Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter to show strong blocking
Added وا to show multiple people did this action. This group is the same 'they' that Allah increased in punishment (زِدْنٰهُمْ) in this verse.

steps.m: and they blocked forcefully (connected to كَفَرُوا۟)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this action with the previous word كَفَرُوا۟ (they disbelieved). Shows these are the same people doing both actions.

steps.m: away from (something moving away from سَبِيلِ)
steps.n: This is a small word that shows something is moving away from something else. In this ayah, it shows people turning away from the path (سَبِيلِ).

steps.m: to travel or follow a path

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes this a past tense verb
Changes from verb 'to travel' to noun 'path' by adding ي
The kasra at the end shows this is Allah's path, connecting it to the word اللَّهِ that comes after it

steps.m: a deity, one who is worshipped
steps.n: Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Added kasra at the end to show possession/belonging

steps.m: to increase or add to something

steps.n: Changed to command form by dropping ي and making it short and sharp with sukun
Added نَا at the end to change from 'you increase!' to 'we increased'

steps.m: we increased them (referring to the disbelievers mentioned in ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who received the action, with an elongated alif (ٰ) before it. This refers back to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to punish harshly or cause pain

steps.n: Added ا after second letter to turn the action into a noun meaning the result of punishment

steps.m: a punishment (object of زِدْنٰهُمْ - We increased them)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the thing being increased in the phrase "We increased them [in] punishment"
Connected the letters with fatha-sukun pattern to create a noun meaning 'above' or 'top'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is describing 'the punishment' (العذاب) in the phrase 'punishment above punishment'
Changed from action to the result of that action by adding alif after second root letter
Added ال to make it specific punishment being discussed
Added kasra at the end because this word comes after فَوْقَ (meaning 'above/over') in the ayah
Basic preposition that shows cause or means
Added kasra to help with pronunciation when connecting to next word

steps.m: because of what (they were doing يُفْسِدُونَ)
steps.n: Added ما which means 'what'. Together with بِ it forms a phrase meaning 'because of what'. In this ayah, it refers to what they were doing (يُفْسِدُونَ - they were corrupting)
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he was'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in the middle of a past tense verb

steps.m: they were (referring to those who disbelieved)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to change 'he' to 'they' (masculine plural). This word refers back to الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ (those who disbelieved) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word describes what the disbelievers (mentioned by كَانُوا) were doing.
Added vowels to the root letters to make the word mean 'a day'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of نَبْعَثُ (we will raise/resurrect)

steps.m: and day (as the object of نَبْعَثُ)
steps.n: Added و with fatha to connect this word to what came before it
Added vowels to connect the root letters into past tense form
Adding ن at start shows 'we' are doing the action

steps.m: in (location within كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ - every nation)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it shows that something is located within each nation.

steps.m: in (location within كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ - every nation)
steps.n: The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this connects to the word شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it, meaning 'of every thing'
Added damma and doubled the middle letter to create the base noun form
Added ة to make it feminine and change meaning from 'mother' to 'nation'
Added two dots under the word (tanween) to show it belongs to 'every' in the phrase 'every nation'
Added ي between second and third letters and ِ on middle letter to show someone who does this action a lot

steps.m: a witness (object of نَبْعَثُ - we raise)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is what is being raised/sent in the sentence

steps.m: upon (preposition related to نَبْعَثُ - we raise)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else. In this ayah, it connects to the verb نَبْعَثُ (we raise).
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns at the end. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added هِم at the end to show this means 'them' (referring to a group of people). This connects back to أُمَّةٍ (nation/community) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
This is a preposition (shows relationship between words) with no root. It has a kasra under the meem.
Added shadda on meem because it comes after the word أُمَّةٍ which ends in a similar sound. This makes it easier to pronounce.
Added sukun at the end to show this is the final form. This word connects to أَنفُسِهِمْ (themselves) to show where the witnesses come from.

steps.m: to breathe, to be precious, to be a self

steps.n: Added vowels to make the basic noun meaning 'self' or 'soul'
Changed the word pattern to make it plural by adding أَ at the start and changing the middle vowel to ُ - this is a common way to make broken plurals in Arabic

steps.m: their selves (referring to the people of each nation mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show these are their selves, and ِ before it to connect the words properly
Basic meaning of coming or arriving
The root letters change to make 'came' by adding dots under the first letter and putting a hamza in the middle
Adds نَا to show 'we' are doing the action
Adds و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Added kasra to the letter ب to make it a preposition meaning 'with' or 'by'

steps.m: with you (masculine singular) (object of جِئْنَا - we brought)
steps.n: Added the pronoun كَ which means 'you' (masculine singular). This word is the object of جِئْنَا (we brought) in this ayah.
Basic meaning of witnessing or testifying
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed the word pattern to show someone who does the action of witnessing

steps.m: a witness (object of نَبْعَثُ/we will raise)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object of the verb نَبْعَثُ (we will raise)
This is the basic form showing the concept of being high or above something
Adding ى changes this from a concept into a preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else
The small alif (ٰ) is added to show that the ى is pronounced as a long 'aa' sound, not as a 'y' sound
Starting with the basic pointing word ها, written with an alif above (ٰ) instead of the normal alif
Added ؤُ to show we're talking about multiple things
Added لَآ to make it point to specific things or people

steps.m: these ones (referring to a group being testified against)
steps.n: Added ءِ at the end and ٓ (maddah) after هٰ to complete the word. In this ayah, it refers to the people who will have witnesses brought against them on the Day of Judgment.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
The doubling of ز indicates intensive or repeated descent/sending down
Adding نا makes this first person plural past tense (we)
Added و at the beginning to connect this clause with the previous one, meaning 'and'
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or affecting something else
The ى changes to يْ when adding a suffix
Added كَ at the end to show this affects 'you' (when speaking to one male)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (wrote) to a noun (book) by adding alif

steps.m: the book (object of نَزَّلْنَا - we sent down)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific ('the book') and َ to show it's the object being sent down
Basic meaning of clarity or explanation
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Added ت at beginning and ان at end to make it a noun meaning 'the act of making clear'
Changed the first vowel from 'a' to 'i' and added a stretched 'aa' sound at the end to make it mean 'something that explains'
Added two small fathas at the end to make it 'an explanation' (not specific)
Basic meaning of totality or completeness
The doubled ل shows this means 'completely all' with no exceptions
Gets a kasra sound at the end to connect it to the next word
Adds لِ at start to mean 'for'. This word connects to تِبْيٰنًا (explanation) in the ayah, showing the Book explains everything.
The ل gets doubled (shadda) because it comes after another word ending in a vowel, making it stronger in pronunciation
Basic meaning related to willing or existing

Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) to show this is indefinite (like 'a/an' in English) and is owned by the word كُلِّ (every/all) that comes before it.

steps.m: to guide or show the way
steps.n: Basic meaning of guidance
Adding damma changes it from verb to abstract noun meaning
Adding tanween and alif maqsura makes it indefinite
Added و with fatha at beginning to connect this word with what came before it

steps.m: to show kindness and care for someone
steps.n: Basic meaning of mercy and compassion
Adding vowel marks to create the noun form meaning 'mercy'
The ة at the end makes this refer to one specific instance of mercy

steps.m: and a specific act of mercy
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word with the previous word هُدًى (guidance)

steps.m: and as a mercy (connected to تِبْيٰنًا/explanation)
steps.n: Added tanween (ً) to show this describes how the Quran was sent down, just like تِبْيٰنًا (as an explanation) before it

Changed from a root meaning 'give news' into a noun meaning 'good news' by adding vowel marks
Added ىٰ at the end to make it mean 'good news' specifically (like adding 'the' in English)
Added و at the beginning to connect it to the previous words (هُدًى وَرَحْمَةً) in the ayah
Basic meaning of submission and peace
Changed the word to show someone who does the action of submitting peacefully to Allah
Added ين to show multiple people who submit (Muslims), with َ at the end showing it connects to other words

steps.m: for the Muslims (receiving بُشْرَىٰ - good news)
steps.n: Added لِ at start to show these Muslims are receiving the good news mentioned in the ayah (بُشْرَىٰ)

This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it. In this ayah, it makes the statement 'Allah commands' stronger and more definite. It also causes the word after it (Allah) to end in َ instead of ُ.
Changed إِلَه to اللَّه by adding ال and a shadda, making it a proper name for The One True God

steps.m: Allah (The One True God) (object of إِنَّ)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because Allah is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily). Also changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This word describes what Allah does, as mentioned at the start of the ayah.
The ُ (damma) ending shows this is a present tense verb in its basic form, telling us what Allah does
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the concept of justice. This word is what Allah commands (يَأْمُرُ) in this ayah.
Added ال to make it specific - this refers to justice as a concept
Added بِ (bi) at the start to mean 'with' or 'by' the justice
Added kasra at the end because it follows the preposition بِ. Also changed اَلْ to ٱلْ which is how it's written in the Quran.

steps.m: to do good, to be excellent

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to a noun showing the action itself by adding إِ at start and ا after second root letter
Added ٱلْ to make it specific
Added وَ to connect it with the previous word (العدل)

steps.m: and the doing of good (connected to العدل)
steps.n: Added kasra (ِ) at the end to match العدل since they are connected words in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'the act of giving'
Extended the alif to show it's a long vowel
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to the previous concepts mentioned (justice and good conduct)
This is a special word that doesn't come from a three-letter root. It's used to show possession or ownership.
The word changes to ذِى when it's being used to show possession of something. Here it's showing possession of الْقُرْبَىٰ (close relatives/kinship).
Changes the word from an action into a special type of closeness, particularly about family relationships

steps.m: the relatives (who are mentioned in إِيتَآئِ ذِى)
steps.n: Makes the word specific by adding 'the'. In this ayah, it refers to giving rights to relatives, as shown by the words إِيتَآئِ ذِى (giving to those of) that come before it.
Adds ى at the end to make it a special type of noun that refers to relatives/kinship
Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding fatha marks, meaning 'he prevented'
Added يَ at the start to change from past (he prevented) to present (he prevents)
Added وَ at the beginning to connect it with the previous word يَأْمُرُ (he commands)

steps.m: and he prevents (connected to يَأْمُرُ - he commands)
steps.n: Added alif mark (ىٰ) at the end to show the long vowel sound
This is a basic preposition that shows something is being moved away from or avoided. In this ayah, it connects with يَنْهَىٰ (forbids) to show what Allah forbids us from doing.
Added kasra at the end to help connect smoothly with the next word ٱلْفَحْشَآءِ (immorality)
Added vowels to show this describes something becoming very bad
Changes from 'became bad' (action) to 'shameful things' (noun) by adding اء - like changing 'did bad' to 'bad things'
Makes the word specific by adding ال
Adds kasra at the end because it comes after عَنِ (away from/against) in the ayah

steps.m: to reject or disapprove of something

steps.n: Added مُ at the start to show this is something that people reject or disapprove of, like how 'write' becomes 'written'
Added ال to show we're talking about specific evil things. This word is what Allah forbids (يَنْهَىٰ) in this verse.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to another word (genitive case)
Adding conjunction 'and'

steps.m: to wrongfully exceed limits, to act unjustly
wrongful behavior, injustice (object of يَنْهَىٰ عَنِ)
steps.n: Changes the action into a noun showing the bad behavior itself. This is one of the things that Allah forbids (يَنْهَىٰ means 'He forbids') in this verse.
Adds the ِ ending because it comes after the word عَنِ (meaning 'from/against')
Adds definite article to make the noun definite
Adds وَ (meaning 'and') to connect this with the other bad things Allah forbids in this verse
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changes from past to present tense - the ي at start shows it's happening now instead of before. Same verb for 'giving advice' but shows ongoing action.
Adds كُمْ to show that Allah is giving advice to all of us (multiple people)
This is a basic particle that expresses hope or expectation. It introduces the purpose or hoped outcome of what was mentioned before.

steps.m: so that you all (referring to those being advised in the ayah)
steps.n: Adds 'you all' as the ones being addressed. This connects to the previous part of the ayah where Allah is giving advice, showing the purpose of that advice.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger or more intensive
Adding تَ at the start shows doing the action to oneself
Adding ون changes it from 'he' to 'you all' and makes it present tense - like going from 'he remembered' to 'you all remember'

steps.m: to fulfill or complete something fully

steps.n: Adding أ at start makes it mean 'to make someone do the action'
Adding وا makes it plural - speaking to many people
Added وَ at beginning to connect this command with what came before it

steps.m: to make a promise or agreement

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning the promise itself
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'by/with' and ِ at the end because it's connected to the word Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes after it
Changed to the specific name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters
Added kasra at the end to show possession, shadda on ل for emphasis, and changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word
This is the basic form of the word that means a specific point in time
Adding ا at the end makes it mean 'whenever' instead of just 'at the time'. In this ayah, it connects to عٰهَدتُّمْ (you made a covenant) to say 'whenever you make a covenant'

steps.m: to make a promise or covenant

steps.n: Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Added alif with dagger to show mutual action between two parties

steps.m: you all made a mutual promise
steps.n: Added تُمْ at the end to show that multiple people (you all) did the action in the past
This is the basic negative particle that means 'not' - it's used to negate what comes after it
Added و at the beginning to connect this negative command with the previous positive command (fulfill). This creates a pair of instructions: 'fulfill... and do not break...'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ
Changed to plural 'you' by adding وا at the end
Adding لَا makes it a prohibition - 'do not' do something

steps.m: to make an oath or promise

steps.n: Forms a noun meaning a single oath or promise
Changes from 'yameen' to 'ayman' to show many oaths/promises instead of just one

steps.m: the oaths (object of تَنقُضُوا - do not break)
steps.n: Adds the definite article and accusative case showing this is the object being acted upon in the phrase 'do not break the oaths'

steps.m: to be distant or far away from something

steps.n: Added vowel marks to transform the root into a word meaning 'after'. The concept comes from something being 'distant in time' from something else.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it in the sentence. Here it's specifically talking about 'after the strengthening of oaths'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes verb 'made sure' into noun meaning 'confirmation'
Added هَا to show this belongs to the oaths (الأَيْمٰنَ) mentioned earlier in the verse
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation - fatha on ق and sukun on د
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it

steps.m: to make or set something in a certain way

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added sukun to prepare for adding endings
Added تُم to show many people (you all) did this in the past
Added ُ at the end because this word connects to كَفِيلًا (guarantor) in the ayah
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters with shadda. This is a unique word that refers specifically to The One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being made witness)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object of the verb جَعَلْتُمُ (you have made). Also changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after a word.
This is a common preposition in Arabic meaning 'upon' or 'on'. Since it's a preposition, we start with its basic form rather than a root.
The ى changes to ي with sukun when adding a suffix. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect meaning.
The suffix كُمْ shows this applies to multiple people being spoken to (you all)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Forms an agent noun meaning 'one who guarantees/takes responsibility'

steps.m: a guarantor (object of جَعَلْتُمُ/you have made)
steps.n: The tanween alif shows this word is being acted upon by the verb جَعَلْتُمُ (you have made/appointed)
Basic form of the emphasis particle that introduces important statements
Added kasra (ِ) under the إ to show how it's pronounced
Added shadda (ّ) on ن to show it's pronounced strongly, which helps emphasize the meaning
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added a long vowel mark (small alif) after the second letter to extend the sound
Added 'al' to make it definite - like changing 'god' to 'the god'

steps.m: Allah (as the object of جَعَلْتُمُ - you have made)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' is a unique name, not just 'the god' with changes. It's marked with a fatha (small line) at the end to show it receives the action of 'you have made'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Adds present tense prefix for 3rd person masculine singular
The ُ shows this is the subject (doer) of the verb. In this ayah, it's part of إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ meaning 'Indeed, Allah knows'
This is a relative pronoun that connects to يَعْلَمُ (knows). It means Allah knows 'what' you do. The ما introduces the object of يَعْلَمُ.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the past tense form
Changes from 'he did' in past to 'you do' now by adding تَ at start

steps.m: you (all) do/are doing (referring to multiple people being addressed)
steps.n: Adding ون shows this is addressing multiple people
This is the basic negative particle لا that means 'not'
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This word connects to تَكُونُوا۟ (be) to say 'and do not be'
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added وا at the end to show multiple people are being addressed. This word comes after لا (don't) to make a negative command - 'don't be!'
Added fatha to the letter ك to make it readable
Added ال which makes the following word definite (like 'the' in English)

steps.m: to break apart or unravel something that was joined together

steps.m: to be strong, blessed, or fortunate

Basic meaning showing the concept of increasing
Added vowel marks (fatha) to the root letters to make it mean 'it increased' in the past

steps.m: to unite, join together, lead as a group

Adding مَا to إِنَّ changes it from just emphasizing to both emphasizing and restricting the meaning. It's like changing 'indeed' to 'it is only' in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final و changes to ا in past tense.
Changes from past tense (he tested) to present tense (he tests) by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowels

steps.m: he tests you all (object of إِنَّمَا)

Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Added 'the' at the start to make it specific

Added pronoun هِ meaning 'it/him' to show what is being referred to
Added small alif (ۦ) after هِ as a pronunciation helper

steps.m: to make something clear or distinct

Doubling the middle letter shows doing the action to others or making it happen
Changes from past to present tense - 'explained' becomes 'explains'
The لَ adds strong emphasis about the future
The نَّ at the end adds extra emphasis to show this is absolutely certain to happen

steps.m: and he will surely make clear

Added tanween to show this is the object of the verb لَجَعَلَكُمْ (He would have made you)
